articles.title: Iraq security forces arrest several officials in anticorruption crackdown
articles.summary: Large-scale operation carried out at dawn in Green Zone and several neighbourhoods in Baghdad, security source says.
articles.titleZh: 伊拉克安全部队在反腐败行动中逮捕了几名官员
articles.summaryZh: 安全消息人士称，黎明时分，在巴格达的绿区和几个街区进行了大规模行动。

contentParagraphs.en: Elite security personnel carry out a large-scale operation at dawn in the Green Zone and several neighbourhoods in Baghdad, security source says.Several Iraqi politicians, lawmakers and officials have been arrested on corruption charges, Iraqi state-run media report.Several people, including members of parliament “whose immunity had been lifted and officials whose names appeared in … confessions”, were arrested early on Sunday in the capital, Baghdad, the Iraqi News Agency reported, quoting a security source.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3Trump blames Iran for ‘foolish’ strike on cargo ship in Strait of Hormuzlist 2 of 3‘Lion of Mesopotamia’: How Aymen Hussein beat tragedy to reach World Cuplist 3 of 3‘Support every Arab team’: In New York, the World Cup unites all Arab fansend of listIt was not immediately clear who had been arrested. There was no immediate official statement on the arrests from the Iraqi government or security forces.A security source told Al Jazeera that elite Iraqi security forces carried out a large-scale arrest operation at dawn in the fortified International Zone (Green Zone) and several neighbourhoods in Baghdad.The source said the arrests were carried out by the Counter Terrorism Service and were based on statements provided by Adnan al-Jumaili, deputy oil minister, after his arrest last month on corruption charges.Iraq’s new prime minister, Ali al-Zaidi, has pledged to fight corruption and mismanagement that have plagued Iraq for decades.Authorities seized about $86m in cash this month that was allegedly part of the corruption case against al-Jumaili.The Associated Press news agency reported that seven people were arrested on Sunday, including five members of parliament. It cited a security agency report it obtained. The AP said some of those arrested were from the political bloc of former Prime Minister Mohammed Shia al-Sudani.During November’s parliamentary elections, al-Sudani’s bloc won the largest share of seats, but he did not return as prime minister. He stepped aside amid a deadlock in the Coordination Framework, a group of Shia parties allied with Iran that brought al-Sudani to power. They disagreed for months over their preferred candidate for the post.
contentParagraphs.zh: 安全消息人士称，精锐安全人员黎明时分在巴格达的绿区和几个街区开展了大规模行动。据伊拉克国营媒体报道，几名伊拉克政界人士、立法者和官员因腐败指控被捕。据伊拉克通讯社援引安全消息人士报道，包括“豁免权已被取消的议会议员和名字出现在……供词中”的官员在内的数人于周日早些时候在首都巴格达被捕。 3 itemslist 1 of 3 特朗普指责伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡对货船进行“愚蠢”袭击list 2 of 3“美索不达米亚之狮”：艾曼·侯赛因如何战胜悲剧进入世界杯 list 3 of 3“支持每支阿拉伯球队”：在纽约，世界杯将所有阿拉伯球迷团结起来 目前还不清楚谁被捕了。伊拉克政府或安全部队没有立即就逮捕行动发表正式声明。一名安全消息人士告诉半岛电视台，伊拉克精锐安全部队黎明时分在戒备森严的国际区（绿区）和巴格达的几个街区进行了大规模逮捕行动。消息人士称，逮捕行动是由反恐部门实施的，并基于石油副部长阿德南·朱迈利上个月因腐败指控被捕后提供的声明。阿里·扎伊迪 (Ali al-Zaidi) 承诺打击困扰伊拉克数十年的腐败和管理不善问题。本月，当局查获了约 8600 万美元现金，据称这些现金是针对阿尔·朱迈利腐败案的一部分。美联社报道称，周日有 7 人被捕，其中包括 5 名议会议员。它引用了其获得的一份安全机构报告。美联社称，其中一些被捕者来自前总理穆罕默德·希亚·苏达尼的政治集团。在11月的议会选举中，苏达尼的集团赢得了最大份额的席位，但他没有以总理身份重返。他在协调框架陷入僵局时下台，协调框架是一群与伊朗结盟的什叶派政党，将苏丹尼推上了权力宝座。几个月来，他们对自己首选的该职位候选人意见不一。

articles.title: Tech firms are blaming AI for mega device and console price rises
articles.summary: Xbox consoles, Nintendo's new Switch 2 and Valve's Steam Deck are just some of the gadgets seeing price hikes in recent months.
articles.titleZh: 科技公司将大型设备和游戏机价格上涨归咎于人工智能
articles.summaryZh: Xbox 游戏机、任天堂的新 Switch 2 和 Valve 的 Steam Deck 只是近几个月价格上涨的一些小玩意。

contentParagraphs.en: For years, buyers of tech could rely on a familiar trend - that older devices would get cheaper over time.That now seems to have stopped, or in some cases, completely reversed.Apple and Microsoft's Xbox have joined the firms hiking prices for devices and games consoles which are years old.They and other tech companies have pointed to the rising cost of crucial components needed to build their machines, laying the blame on AI.Compute-hungry data centres, which power AI, need more and more chips to keep up with demand from AI companies - which means the demand for them is far outstripping supply.Some people have called it "Ramageddon" - as random access memory (Ram), a once-cheap part of any computer, has now shot up in price.But big tech's Ram woes are failing to gain the sympathy of many consumers facing eye-watering costs for barely-new electronics.Apple has raised the prices of its tablets and laptops by nearly 20%.The news was swiftly followed by Microsoft saying it would yet again raise the price of its five-year-old Xbox Series S and X consoles by at least $100 (£75.70).The pricing changes, which will take effect from August, are its third in just over a year - and see the cost of a new console be 30% to 40% more expensive than it was this time last year."Xbox with another hardware price increase? I gotta laugh to keep from crying," wrote one X user, external reacting to Xbox's console price hikes. "My favorite hobby is cooked."On Reddit, another user responding to the news said Xbox "may as well just cancel" its upcoming console Helix "because no one will be able to afford it".Once an affordable component used to build devices, Ram prices more than doubled in price between October 2025 and the start of 2026."The race to build out AI data centres is resulting in a swift and significant increase in demand that chip makers are rushing to meet," said Danni Hewson, head of financial analysis at investment firm AJ Bell.She said such demand was enabling big chip makers such as TSMC to raise prices "knowing that customers are vying with each other for production capacity".Prices of popular memory kits like DDR4 and its bandwidth-boosting successor the DDR5 have shot up accordingly.According to Counterpoint Research, some 32GB DDR5 components for PCs jumped from $94 in the three months to September 2025 to $127 in the next three months. In the first quarter of 2026, spanning the start January to the end of March, the same components had soared 122% to $282.Since then Dram, which provides a sort of short term memory for devices to facilitate what you do on them in real-time, and Nand flash - long-term memory that stores data even when a device is turned off - have climbed further in price.RSM UK's tech senior analyst James Bull noted that the four largest US tech firms are expected to spend hundreds of billions of dollars on data centres and AI equipment in 2026."That level of demand for memory chips has created a shortage the supply chain cannot keep pace with," he said.With big tech and AI firms buying memory at scale, and able to pay a premium for longer contracts, manufacturers were also being incentivised to prioritise their orders over consumer electronics, Bull added."Essentially, the MacBook on consumers' desks is now competing for the same Dram as the data centres powering ChatGPT and is losing."Of course, some of the big tech companies, such as Microsoft, have a foot in both camps - investing billions in AI infrastructure while also making consumer products such as the Xbox.But the memory shortage and price rises have also been compounded by inflation and geopolitical issues, say some.When Sony announced further price increases to the PS5 console in the UK and elsewhere around the world, it cited "continued pressures in the global economic landscape".Piers Harding-Rolls of Ampere Analysis said at the time that alongside rising Ram prices, further waves of inflation linked to the war in Iran might have influenced the scale of Sony's price increases.Hewson told the BBC more recent price hikes could "go even higher as chip makers deal with increased costs resulting from the blockade in the Strait of Hormuz". "Whilst the last few days have brought renewed optimism that the situation in the Middle East is being resolved, the impact of the last couple of months means some inflation is now baked in," she added.
contentParagraphs.zh: 多年来，科技买家可能会依赖一个熟悉的趋势——随着时间的推移，旧设备会变得更便宜。现在这种情况似乎已经停止，或者在某些情况下完全逆转。苹果和微软的 Xbox 也加入了这些公司的行列，提高了老旧设备和游戏机的价格。他们和其他科技公司指出，构建机器所需的关键组件的成本不断上涨，将责任归咎于人工智能。为人工智能提供动力的计算密集型数据中心需要越来越多的芯片来满足人工智能公司的需求——这这意味着对它们的需求远远超过了供应。有人称之为“Ramageddon”——因为随机存取存储器 (Ram)，曾经是任何计算机的廉价部件，现在价格已经飙升。但大型科技公司的 RAM 困境并没有赢得许多消费者的同情，这些消费者面临着新电子产品的令人眼花缭乱的成本。苹果已将其平板电脑和笔记本电脑的价格提高了近 20%。微软紧随其后，表示将再次提高价格已经使用五年的 Xbox Series S 和 X 游戏机价格至少上涨 100 美元（75.70 英镑）。此次价格调整将于 8 月生效，这是一年多以来的第三次调整，新游戏机的成本比去年同期贵了 30% 至 40%。“Xbox 硬件价格再次上涨？我必须笑才能不哭，”一位 X 用户在对 Xbox 游戏机价格上涨的外部反应中写道。 “我最喜欢的爱好是烹饪。”在 Reddit 上，另一位回应这一消息的用户表示，Xbox“可能会取消”即将推出的游戏机 Helix，“因为没有人买得起”。曾经是用于构建设备的廉价组件，Ram 的价格在 2025 年 10 月至 2026 年初之间上涨了一倍多。“建设人工智能数据中心的竞赛导致芯片制造商急于满足的需求迅速而显着增加，”丹尼说。投资公司 AJ Bell 的财务分析主管休森表示，这种需求使得台积电等大型芯片制造商能够提高价格，“因为他们知道客户正在相互争夺产能”。DDR4 及其带宽提升后继产品 DDR5 等流行内存套件的价格也相应上涨。根据 Counterpoint Research 的数据，截至 2025 年 9 月的三个月内，一些用于 PC 的 32GB DDR5 组件从 94 美元上涨到未来三个月 127 美元。 2026 年第一季度，从 1 月初到 3 月底，这些组件的价格飙升了 122%，达到 282 美元。此后，Dram（为设备提供一种短期存储器，方便您在设备上实时执行操作）和 Nand 闪存（即使设备关闭时也能存储数据的长期存储器）的价格进一步攀升。RSM UK 的科技高级分析师 James Bull 指出，美国四大科技公司预计将在到 2026 年，数据中心和人工智能设备将花费数千亿美元。Bull 补充道，“对内存芯片的需求水平造成了供应链无法跟上的短缺。”随着大型科技和人工智能公司大规模购买内存，并且能够为更长的合同支付溢价，制造商也受到激励，将订单优先于消费电子产品。“从本质上讲，消费者办公桌上的 MacBook 现在正在与为 ChatGPT 供电的数据中心争夺相同的 Dram，并且当然，一些大型科技公司，比如微软，在这两个阵营中都有立足之地——在人工智能基础设施上投资数十亿美元，同时也生产 Xbox 等消费产品。但一些人表示，通货膨胀和地缘政治问题也加剧了内存短缺和价格上涨。当索尼宣布进一步提高英国和世界其他地方的 PS5 游戏机价格时，它提到了“全球经济格局的持续压力”。安培分析公司的皮尔斯·哈丁-罗尔斯当时表示， RAM价格上涨、与伊朗战争相关的进一步通胀可能影响了索尼涨价的规模。休森告诉BBC，“随着芯片制造商应对霍尔木兹海峡封锁导致的成本增加，最近的涨价可能会更高”。她补充说：“虽然过去几天人们重新乐观地认为中东局势正在得到解决，但过去几个月的影响意味着一些通货膨胀现在已经开始显现。”

articles.title: This mindset shift can help you get better at using up your leftovers
articles.summary: Don't let your leftovers go to waste. Cookbook authors share clever storage techniques — like an "Eat Me First" box in your fridge — and cooking tricks to help you make the most of your food scraps.
articles.titleZh: 这种思维方式的转变可以帮助您更好地利用剩菜剩饭
articles.summaryZh: 不要让你的剩菜浪费掉。烹饪书作者分享了巧妙的存储技巧——比如冰箱里的“先吃我”盒子——以及帮助你充分利用食物残渣的烹饪技巧。

contentParagraphs.en: If you're struggling to use up leftovers like a half-eaten rotisserie chicken, turn the assignment into a creative exercise, says chef Margaret Li. It'll make the cooking process more fun and less guilt-driven.
contentParagraphs.zh: 厨师玛格丽特·李 (Margaret Li) 表示，如果你像吃完一半的烤鸡一样难以吃完剩菜，请将这项任务变成一项创造性的练习。它会让烹饪过程变得更加有趣，减少负罪感。

contentParagraphs.en: Pulse/Getty Images/Corbis RF Stills
contentParagraphs.zh: Pulse/Getty Images/Corbis RF Stills

contentParagraphs.en: Pulse/Getty Images/Corbis RF Stills
contentParagraphs.zh: Pulse/Getty Images/Corbis RF Stills

contentParagraphs.en: On a recent weeknight, I opened up my fridge and found an assortment of half-eaten or ignored food.
contentParagraphs.zh: 最近一个工作日的晚上，我打开冰箱，发现了各种各样吃了一半或被忽视的食物。

contentParagraphs.en: That included takeout that I didn't find appetizing enough to eat for lunch. A rotisserie chicken with most of the meat picked off. A couple of raw vegetables from the farmers market that were starting to wilt.
contentParagraphs.zh: 其中包括我觉得午餐不够开胃的外卖。一只烤鸡，大部分肉都被去掉了。农贸市场上的一些生蔬菜开始枯萎。

contentParagraphs.en: "There's nothing to eat," I told myself. Yet even I knew that was ridiculous. There was plenty of food in my fridge. I just didn't feel inspired to cook with it.
contentParagraphs.zh: “没有什么可吃的，”我告诉自己。但即使是我也知道这很荒谬。我的冰箱里有很多食物。我只是没有灵感用它做饭。

contentParagraphs.en: So I asked some chefs for guidance. How could I more consistently use leftovers and the other ingredients I tend to overlook?
contentParagraphs.zh: 于是我请教了一些厨师。我怎样才能更一致地使用剩菜和我容易忽视的其他成分？

contentParagraphs.en: Start with a mindset shift, says Margaret Li, chef and co-author of the cookbook Perfectly Good Food: A Totally Achievable Zero Waste Approach to Home Cooking. Think about cooking with leftovers as a creative, experimental exercise, not a guilt-driven one.
contentParagraphs.zh: 厨师兼烹饪书《完美美食：完全可实现的零浪费家庭烹饪方法》的合著者玛格丽特·李 (Margaret Li) 表示，首先要转变心态。将用剩菜做饭视为一种创造性的实验性练习，而不是一种内疚感驱动的练习。

contentParagraphs.en: "It ends up being this fun game where you are creating something from what seems like nothing and solving this puzzle, and then you get to eat it," she says.
contentParagraphs.zh: “这最终成为了一个有趣的游戏，你可以从看似无的东西中创造出一些东西，并解决这个难题，然后你就可以吃掉它，”她说。

contentParagraphs.en: There are other good reasons to use up your food scraps. Nationally, about a quarter of food products go to waste, according to the nonprofit ReFED. In my own household, where we spend about $200 a week on groceries, that means I might be throwing out the equivalent of $50 of food — an unnecessary burden on my wallet, not to mention the environment.
contentParagraphs.zh: 用完食物残渣还有其他充分的理由。据非营利组织 ReFED 称，在全国范围内，大约四分之一的食品被浪费了。在我自己的家里，我们每周在杂货上花费约 200 美元，这意味着我可能会扔掉相当于 50 美元的食物——这对我的钱包来说是不必要的负担，更不用说环境了。

contentParagraphs.en: The chefs I spoke to had some practical tips about using up more of the food we buy. Here are a few that I put to the test.
contentParagraphs.zh: 我采访过的厨师有一些关于消耗更多我们购买的食物的实用技巧。以下是我测试过的一些内容。

contentParagraphs.en: Find your "hero recipes"
contentParagraphs.zh: 找到你的“英雄食谱”

contentParagraphs.en: Build up an arsenal of go-to recipes that are flexible enough to use up just about any ingredient. Li calls them "hero recipes."
contentParagraphs.zh: 建立一个常用食谱库，这些食谱足够灵活，可以使用几乎任何成分。李称它们为“英雄食谱”。

contentParagraphs.en: I tried one of these from her cookbook, called "Make-It-Your-Own Stir-Fry." (Scroll down for the recipe.) It includes loose ingredients like "1 pound crisp-crunchy vegetables" or "4 cups leafy greens."
contentParagraphs.zh: 我尝试了她的食谱中的其中一种，叫做“自己做炒菜”。 （向下滚动查看食谱。）它包括松散的成分，例如“1 磅脆脆蔬菜”或“4 杯绿叶蔬菜”。

contentParagraphs.en: In the spirit of the recipe, I pulled vegetables out of my fridge at random and did not measure them out. The sauce was a simple mixture of soy sauce, vinegar, sugar and water. By the time I topped my bowl with chopped scallions, the dish looked like a gourmet meal, not an afterthought.
contentParagraphs.zh: 本着食谱的精神，我随机从冰箱里取出蔬菜，没有量出它们。酱汁是酱油、醋、糖和水的简单混合物。当我在碗里放上切碎的葱时，这道菜看起来就像一顿美食，而不是事后的想法。

contentParagraphs.en: Other ideas: "You could put anything in a frittata, and it'll be great," says Tamar Adler, chef and author of The Everlasting Meal Cookbook: Leftovers A-Z.
contentParagraphs.zh: 其他想法：“你可以在菜肉馅煎蛋饼中放入任何东西，这会很棒，”主厨兼《永恒膳食食谱：剩菜剩饭 A-Z》的作者塔玛尔·阿德勒 (Tamar Adler) 说。

contentParagraphs.en: Or, if you have day-old rice on hand, cook it alongside other ingredients to make fried rice. "Saute some aromatics — ginger, garlic, onion — in oil," Adler says. Then add your rice and whatever leftover bits you have, like the rotisserie chicken and older produce I had in my fridge.
contentParagraphs.zh: 或者，如果您手头有一天的米饭，请将其与其他配料一起煮成炒饭。 “在油中炒一些芳香剂——生姜、大蒜、洋葱，”阿德勒说。然后加入米饭和任何剩下的东西，比如烤鸡和我冰箱里的旧农产品。

contentParagraphs.en: "Just take the approach of making it more flavorful and crispy and then spicy, and then usually adding a squeeze of lemon," Adler says.
contentParagraphs.zh: “只需采取使其更美味、更脆、然后辣的方法，然后通常添加一点柠檬汁，”阿德勒说。

contentParagraphs.en: Label your leftovers
contentParagraphs.zh: 给你的剩菜贴上标签

contentParagraphs.en: Keep a permanent marker and painter's tape in your kitchen to label and date your leftovers, Li says. "That is a classic chef's method for knowing what something is and when it was made. That saves you the guessing game."
contentParagraphs.zh: 李说，在你的厨房里放一个永久记号笔和油漆胶带，给你的剩菜贴上标签并注明日期。 “这是厨师的经典方法，可以了解某物是什么以及何时制作。这可以让你省去猜谜游戏。”

contentParagraphs.en: Adler takes the concept a step further and labels her leftovers with their intended use. Leftover blueberries are labeled "muffins-to-be on Tuesday," she says. "I really like doing that — assigning the destiny of the food."
contentParagraphs.zh: 阿德勒将这个概念更进一步，并用其预期用途标记了她的剩菜。她说，剩下的蓝莓被贴上“周二松饼”的标签。 “我真的很喜欢这样做——分配食物的命运。”

contentParagraphs.en: So after a night of Ethiopian takeout, when we ended up with an entire container of leftover injera, I followed Adler's advice and thought about what it might become in the future.
contentParagraphs.zh: 因此，在吃完一晚埃塞俄比亚外卖后，当我们吃完一整箱剩下的因吉拉时，我听从了阿德勒的建议，思考了它未来可能会变成什么样子。

contentParagraphs.en: I imagined scrambling the spongy, tangy bread with eggs, akin to scrambling matzo into matzo brei. "Injera for eggs," I wrote on the container. Sure enough, their destiny was fulfilled the following morning.
contentParagraphs.zh: 我想象着将松软、味道浓郁的面包与鸡蛋搅拌，类似于将无酵饼炒成无酵饼。 “鸡蛋因吉拉”，我在容器上写下。果然，第二天早上他们的命运就实现了。

contentParagraphs.en: Li keeps a dedicated bag in her freezer just for scraps from which to make chicken or vegetable stock. That bag houses carrot peels, the ends of onions, extra garlic cloves and chicken bones.
contentParagraphs.zh: 李在她的冰箱里放了一个专门的袋子，专门用来存放用来制作鸡肉或蔬菜汤的残渣。那个袋子里装着胡萝卜皮、洋葱头、额外的蒜瓣和鸡骨头。

contentParagraphs.en: Don't forget your odds and ends
contentParagraphs.zh: 不要忘记你的零碎

contentParagraphs.en: Adler encouraged me to never, ever throw away the stems of herbs. Stems don't get as much glory as tender, pretty leaves, but they still have the same herby taste.
contentParagraphs.zh: 阿德勒鼓励我永远不要扔掉药草的茎。茎不像嫩、漂亮的叶子那样光彩夺目，但它们仍然具有相同的草本味道。

contentParagraphs.en: "I'm going to chop these herbs up or stick them in a blender with a clove of garlic," she says. Then add olive oil. "And then it's just gonna be my base sauce for everything."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我要把这些香草切碎，或者把它们和一瓣大蒜一起放进搅拌机里，”她说。然后加入橄榄油。 “然后它就会成为我一切的基础酱料。”

contentParagraphs.en: So I foraged a few varieties of half-cut herbs from my refrigerator drawers, most of them sad looking and unidentifiable.
contentParagraphs.zh: 因此，我从冰箱抽屉里找出了几种半切的草药，其中大多数看起来很悲伤且无法辨认。

contentParagraphs.en: I threw out the stems that had turned brown and gooey and put the rest in a blender. I added garlic on Adler's instructions, nuts and kale for bulk, and plenty of olive oil and salt. Then, on a whim, I added a splash of olive juice for brightness.
contentParagraphs.zh: 我扔掉了变成棕色和粘糊糊的茎，把剩下的放进搅拌机里。我按照阿德勒的指示添加了大蒜、坚果和羽衣甘蓝，以及大量的橄榄油和盐。然后，我一时兴起，加了一点橄榄汁来提亮。

contentParagraphs.en: The result was somewhere between a pesto and a chimichurri, and it elevated that night's otherwise routine dinner. And Adler was right: Once the stems were blended, it tasted exactly the same as the leaves. (The same idea applies for broccoli stems in a cheesy broccoli soup, Li says.)
contentParagraphs.zh: 结果介于香蒜酱和奇米丘里酱之间，它提高了当晚原本例行公事的晚餐的质量。阿德勒是对的：一旦茎混合在一起，它的味道就和叶子一模一样。 （李说，同样的想法也适用于奶酪西兰花汤中的西兰花茎。）

contentParagraphs.en: Li likes to keep her odds and ends organized with an "Eat Me First" box in her fridge. That's where she keeps half-used lemons, leftover coconut milk or produce that's starting to get wrinkly. "You kind of have an idea for, OK, here's where you look first," she says.
contentParagraphs.zh: 李喜欢在冰箱里放一个“先吃我”的盒子，把她的零碎物品整理得井井有条。她在那里存放用了一半的柠檬、剩下的椰奶或开始起皱的农产品。 “你有一个想法，好吧，这就是你首先看的地方，”她说。

contentParagraphs.en: Don't strive for perfection
contentParagraphs.zh: 不要追求完美

contentParagraphs.en: Cooking these meals did feel like a game, as Li had suggested. It brought me unexpected joy to use up as many existing ingredients as possible — to the point where I often spent much longer in the kitchen because I kept thinking of new ideas: If I turn these wrinkly sweet potatoes into a soup, then I can caramelize this half-cut onion for a topping, and then I can use the leftover soup as a sauce tomorrow ...
contentParagraphs.zh: 正如李所说，做饭确实感觉像一场游戏。尽可能多地使用现有的食材给我带来了意想不到的快乐——以至于我经常在厨房待更长时间，因为我不断思考新的想法：如果我把这些皱巴巴的红薯做成汤，然后我可以把这个半切的洋葱焦糖化，然后明天我可以用剩下的汤作为酱汁……

contentParagraphs.en: Did I cook more often, though? Probably n
contentParagraphs.zh: 不过，我做饭的次数多了吗？可能是n

articles.title: I hear my son crying beneath the rubble, says Venezuela earthquake survivor
articles.summary: Andreina Valerio says she fears for her son, who is trapped with her partner and in-laws beneath a collapsed building.
articles.titleZh: 委内瑞拉地震幸存者说，我听到我儿子在废墟下哭泣
articles.summaryZh: 安德烈娜·瓦莱里奥 (Andreina Valerio) 说，她担心儿子和她的伴侣以及公婆被困在一栋倒塌的建筑物下。

contentParagraphs.en: On the day that two devastating earthquakes struck Venezuela, Andreina Valerio rushed back from work to look for her almost two-year-old son, Santiago.He was with Andreina's partner, Ramsés Mendoza, at her in-laws' home in La Guaira.When she got there, she found the building in ruins. Her brother-in-law Samuel Mendoza was looking through the pile of rubble that used to be their apartment block. When I met Andreina outside a crumbling building on Saturday, she told me her son and partner were still trapped inside, along with her partner's mother, father, grandparents and sister. But she had not given up hope. Andreina and Samuel told me there were other children trapped in the building, too - a nine-year-old boy named Lucas and a three-year-old girl named Aranza.Rescue teams from El Salvador and Spain arrived at the scene on Saturday, but were unable to get inside. No one had been rescued from the building by that point.The first morning after the earthquakes, Samuel said he "heard a woman's voice, someone whose voice I couldn't understand at first, and the only word was 'help'."The next day, when Andreina went back, she heard a baby crying."I still have faith my son is alive," she said. "I have faith that it's my son. And I know my son will get through this, as will his family."Andreina and Samuel are two of many Venezuelans searching for loved ones in the rubble, holding out hope that they can be rescued after two massive earthquakes levelled hundreds of buildings across the country.Families are digging in the rubble with their bare hands in La Guaira, the coastal region which is one of the areas most badly affected.Those I spoke to were sleep deprived, their voices hoarse from screaming for survivors.As the hours passed on Friday, neighbours started to help, and people from other parts of Venezuela arrived to provide support.Rescue teams are working hard across the country, but it seems Venezuela is unprepared to handle the earthquakes - and this is a country that has dealt with extreme crises for years.When I arrived at the Hotel Edward, I could smell the death. You could feel that it would get worse over time. It seemed impossible that people would survive for long.I saw more than 50 damaged buildings in the town of La Guaira. Official figures show more than 1,400 structures have been affected in the region.Jorge Rodríguez, president of Venezuela's National Assembly, says the earthquakes are "the most disastrous event this republic has suffered in the last 123 years". The official death toll for the country has risen to 1,430, with 3,238 injured. But with tens of thousands still reported missing, emergency services are working around the clock to find survivors.Several police and military officers could be seen on the streets of La Guaira. Interim President Delcy Rodríguez has said 14,000 were sent to the state, which she says has been "militarised" for safety.At first, I could see just one small tractor on the street. Heavy machinery later appeared after what must have been a difficult journey via a small, broken road.As the hours passed, some told the screaming families to be quiet, but their pleas were drowned out by the crowds and motorcycles.Volunteers handed out medicine to anyone who needed it, and clothes were left in piles on the ground in the hopes they would be given to people in need.Delcy Rodríguez has said rescue teams from 10 nations were expected to arrive in La Guaira state on Saturday. Electricity in the region had been restored to 60%, she added.People have been warned no to travel to La Guaira, with Jorge Rodríguez saying it has suffered "tremendous devastation".At a hospital in Caracas, which has received injured people from La Guaira in recent days, I spoke with a doctor who said at least 600 people had been brought there, most of them with fractures.He also said patients had been traumatised, with some experiencing panic attacks.A list of the dead and people being treated is displayed outside, in case people are looking there for their loved ones.Some people have made posters of missing relatives and pinned them up next to the list.Additional reporting by Ian Aikman.
contentParagraphs.zh: QUERY LENGTH LIMIT EXCEEDED. MAX ALLOWED QUERY : 500 CHARS

articles.title: July 4th fireworks in Washington: What's different for America's 250th?
articles.summary: This year's fireworks display in Washington, D.C., is scheduled toWhat to know about the massive July 4 firework show for America's 250th
articles.titleZh: 7 月 4 日华盛顿烟花表演：美国第 250 届烟花表演有何不同？
articles.summaryZh: 今年在华盛顿特区举行的烟花表演定于 7 月 4 日举行的美国建国 250 周年大型烟花表演了解一下

contentParagraphs.en: Fireworks illuminate the skyline above the Lincoln Memorial, Washington Monument and U.S. Capitol during U.S. Independence Day celebrations, as seen from Arlington, Va., on July 4, 2025.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2025 年 7 月 4 日从弗吉尼亚州阿灵顿看到的美国独立日庆祝活动期间，烟花照亮了林肯纪念堂、华盛顿纪念碑和美国国会大厦上方的天际线。

contentParagraphs.en: Mohammad Reza Mousavi/Middle East Images/AFP via Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 穆罕默德·雷扎·穆萨维/中东图片/法新社盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: Mohammad Reza Mousavi/Middle East Images/AFP via Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 穆罕默德·雷扎·穆萨维/中东图片/法新社盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: The Trump administration is promising a spectacle like no other on July 4. A massive fireworks display in Washington, D.C., will celebrate America's 250th birthday — and could also break a world record.
contentParagraphs.zh: 特朗普政府承诺 7 月 4 日将呈现一场独一无二的奇观。在华盛顿特区举行的大型烟花表演将庆祝美国 250 岁生日，而且还可能打破世界纪录。

contentParagraphs.en: President Trump says he will personally launch "the LARGEST FIREWORKS SHOW IN HISTORY" at the event.
contentParagraphs.zh: 特朗普总统表示，他将亲自在活动中启动“历史上最大的烟花表演”。

contentParagraphs.en: Setting a new Guinness World Record was a top priority for the event's organizers at the Freedom 250 organization, a White House commission. And Pyrotecnico, the company putting on the July 4th show, plans to do that. But the company's CEO, Stephen Vitale, also has another goal.
contentParagraphs.zh: 创造新的吉尼斯世界纪录是白宫委员会“自由 250”组织此次活动组织者的首要任务。举办 7 月 4 日展会的 Pyrotecnico 公司也计划这么做。但该公司首席执行官史蒂芬·维塔莱(Stephen Vitale)还有另一个目标。

contentParagraphs.en: "Our main focus is to make this the most memorable fireworks display that this generation will have ever seen," he says.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们的主要目标是让这场烟花表演成为这一代人所见过的最令人难忘的烟花表演，”他说。

contentParagraphs.en: To do that, dozens of Pyrotecnico technicians will fire off about 851,000 fireworks, a number that would eclipse what is usually the biggest annual July 4 show in the U.S.
contentParagraphs.zh: 为此，数十名 Pyrotecnico 技术人员将燃放约 851,000 枚烟花，这一数字将超过美国 7 月 4 日举行的最大规模的年度烟花表演。

contentParagraphs.en: "Macy's has traditionally been what I call the granddaddy of them all," says Julie Heckman, the executive director of the American Pyrotechnics Association, an industry trade group.
contentParagraphs.zh: “梅西百货传统上被我称为它们的鼻祖，”行业贸易组织美国烟火协会执行董事朱莉·赫克曼 (Julie Heckman) 说。

contentParagraphs.en: "That show traditionally has between 60 and 85,000 aerial shells and effects," she adds. "So D.C. is looking at 10 times that quantity in setting this world record."
contentParagraphs.zh: “该节目传统上有 60 到 85,000 个空中炮弹和特效，”她补充道。 “因此，华盛顿特区希望以 10 倍的数量来创造这项世界纪录。”

contentParagraphs.en: Big changes for a big show in 2026
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年大型演出的重大变化

contentParagraphs.en: President Trump arrives at a rally to kick off the Great American State Fair, which is part of the Freedom 250 celebration, on the National Mall on June 24.
contentParagraphs.zh: 6 月 24 日，特朗普总统抵达国家广场举行的大型美国州博览会开幕集会，该博览会是自由 250 周年庆祝活动的一部分。

contentParagraphs.en: The National Mall show usually starts around 9 p.m. ET and lasts less than 20 minutes. But organizers say this year's show will start around 10:30 p.m. and will be more than twice as long — at 40 minutes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 国家广场表演通常在晚上 9 点左右开始。 ET 持续不到 20 分钟。但组织者表示，今年的展会将于晚上 10:30 左右开始。时长将是原来的两倍多——40 分钟。

contentParagraphs.en: That is shorter than the fireworks show that holds the current Guinness World Record. A megachurch in the Philippines took more than an hour to blast off nearly 811,000 fireworks when it set that record 10 years ago.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这比目前保持着吉尼斯世界纪录的烟花表演还要短。菲律宾的一座大型教堂在 10 年前创下了这一纪录，花了一个多小时才燃放了近 811,000 枚烟花。

contentParagraphs.en: In previous years, Washington's fireworks have been launched from racks set up along the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. But this year, Vitale says there are many more launch locations.
contentParagraphs.zh: 往年，华盛顿的烟花都是从林肯纪念堂倒影池沿岸设置的烟花架上燃放的。但维塔莱表示，今年有更多的发射地点。

contentParagraphs.en: "It's going to be fireworks in stereo," he says.
contentParagraphs.zh: “这将是立体声烟花，”他说。

contentParagraphs.en: In 2026, fireworks will be shot off from sites that stretch from the National Mall to nearby West Potomac Park and on to eight barges floating in the Potomac River.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年，烟花将从国家广场延伸至附近的西波托马克公园，并在波托马克河上漂浮的八艘驳船上燃放。

contentParagraphs.en: Audiences will see lots of red, white and blue, and hear patriotic songs and pop music.
contentParagraphs.zh: 观众将看到大量的红、白、蓝，并听到爱国歌曲和流行音乐。

contentParagraphs.en: Vitale says the longer run time should give smoke time to dissipate between elements. But Washington's weather can also influence that. Julie Heckman says high humidity can literally put a damper on fireworks shows.
contentParagraphs.zh: 维塔莱表示，较长的运行时间应该可以让烟雾有时间在元素之间消散。但华盛顿的天气也会影响这一点。朱莉·赫克曼说，高湿度确实会阻碍烟花表演。

contentParagraphs.en: "We hope that we don't have [a night] where the humidity kind of hangs the smoke" and clouds people's views, Heckman says.
contentParagraphs.zh: 赫克曼说：“我们希望[夜晚]不会因为湿度而产生烟雾”并遮蔽人们的视线。

contentParagraphs.en: And some things will remain the same
contentParagraphs.zh: 有些事情将保持不变

contentParagraphs.en: An aerial view of the National Mall on the first day of the Great American State Fair on June 25 in Washington, D.C.
contentParagraphs.zh: 6 月 25 日，华盛顿特区，伟大美国州博览会第一天的国家广场鸟瞰图。

contentParagraphs.en: Win McNamee/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 赢得麦克纳米/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: Win McNamee/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 赢得麦克纳米/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: The scale of the D.C. show is massive, but its largest fireworks shells will be 10 inches, the same as previous years. That size conforms with safety codes for the relatively constrained area of the National Mall.
contentParagraphs.zh: 华盛顿特区的烟花表演规模宏大，但最大的烟花弹将达到10英寸，与往年相同。该尺寸符合国家广场相对受限区域的安全规范。

contentParagraphs.en: The National Fire Protection Association code for public display includes a formula for creating safe buffer zones. Explaining the math, Heckman says that a shell can travel 100 feet in the air for every inch of its size. And that determines how far away spectators must be.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国国家消防协会的公共展示规范包括创建安全缓冲区的公式。赫克曼在解释数学时表示，炮弹每增加一英寸大小，就可以在空中飞行 100 英尺。这决定了观众必须保持多远的距离。

contentParagraphs.en: For a 10-inch shell, Vitale says, a 1,000-foot setback and barrier are needed.
contentParagraphs.zh: Vitale 说，对于 10 英寸的炮弹，需要 1,000 英尺的退缩和障碍物。

contentParagraphs.en: Freedom 250 did not respond to NPR questions about how much the display will cost.
contentParagraphs.zh: Freedom 250 没有回答 NPR 关于该显示器的售价的问题。

contentParagraphs.en: An event that 'touches all of our senses'
contentParagraphs.zh: 一场“触动我们所有感官”的活动

contentParagraphs.en: The fireworks display in Washington, D.C., in 2025 lasted less than 20 minutes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2025年华盛顿特区的烟花表演持续了不到20分钟。

contentParagraphs.en: Mehmet Eser/Middle East Images/AFP via Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: Mehmet Eser/中东图片/法新社盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: Mehmet Eser/Middle East Images/AFP via Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: Mehmet Eser/中东图片/法新社盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: Despite using some similar fireworks, Vitale predicts that this year's July 4th show will be louder.
contentParagraphs.zh: 尽管使用了一些类似的烟花，维塔莱预计今年 7 月 4 日的表演将会更加响亮。

contentParagraphs.en: "Because of the volume, it'll certainly pack a larger punch."
contentParagraphs.zh: “由于体积大，它肯定会产生更大的冲击力。”

contentParagraphs.en: And that's the idea: to etch a new memory into the national consciousness.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这就是我们的想法：将新的记忆铭刻在国民意识中。

contentParagraphs.en: "I think that's what they want," Heckman says. "The company — and the president as well, who's putting on the grandest, biggest show ever — really wants the takeaway, that this is the best show you've ever seen."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我认为这就是他们想要的，”赫克曼说。 “公司——还有总统，他正在上演有史以来最盛大、规模最大的演出——真的想要得到一个收获，那就是这是你见过的最好的演出。”

contentParagraphs.en: Vitale says dozens of workers will orchestrate the bursts of color, movement and sound in the show. The fireworks are sourced from around the world, he says, from flower-themed round shells from Asia to cylindrical fireworks from Europe.
contentParagraphs.zh: 维塔莱表示，数十名工作人员将在节目中精心策划色彩、动作和声音的爆发。他说，这些烟花来自世界各地，从亚洲的花卉主题圆形烟花到欧洲的圆柱形烟花。

contentParagraphs.en: "European-style fireworks are incredibly different," he adds, listing elements from loud, sustained crackling sounds to sparkling whirlwinds and "swimming effects" that squiggle across the sky.
contentParagraphs.zh: “欧洲风格的烟花非常不同，”他补充道，列出了从响亮、持续的爆裂声到闪闪发光的旋风和在天空中蜿蜒划过的“游泳效果”等元素。

contentParagraphs.en: "That all matters," Vitale says. "Because, why people love fireworks so much is it really touches all of our senses."
contentParagraphs.zh: “这很重要，”维塔莱说。 “因为，人们之所以如此喜爱烟花，是因为它确实触动了我们所有的感官。”

contentParagraphs.en: If all goes well, people will be talking about this show for 50 years — until the next big anniversary.
contentParagraphs.zh: 如果一切顺利，人们将会谈论这个节目 50 年——直到下一个重要的周年纪念日。

articles.title: Trump administration partially lifts export ban on Anthropic's most advanced AI model
articles.summary: The U.S. government is asserting a new level of influence over AI, controlling which companies can access Anthropic's new models. OpenAI agreed to let the administration screen users of its new model.
articles.titleZh: 特朗普政府部分解除对 Anthropic 最先进人工智能模型的出口禁令
articles.summaryZh: 美国政府正在对人工智能施加新的影响力，控制哪些公司可以访问 Anthropic 的新模型。 OpenAI 同意让政府对其新模型的用户进行筛选。

contentParagraphs.en: The U.S. government is asserting a new level of influence over AI, controlling which companies can access Anthropic's new models. OpenAI agreed to let the administration screen users of its new model.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国政府正在对人工智能施加新的影响力，控制哪些公司可以访问 Anthropic 的新模型。 OpenAI 同意让政府对其新模型的用户进行筛选。

contentParagraphs.en: SCOTT SIMON, HOST: U.S. government's asserting a new level of control over AI. Earlier this month it banned foreigners from using two of Anthropic's most powerful new models. It partially reversed the ban yesterday but retains control over which companies have access. Meanwhile, Anthropic's rival OpenAI said yesterday that it had agreed to let the administration screen users of its newest model. NPR's tech correspondent John Ruwitch joins us. John, thanks so much for being with us.JOHN RUWITCH, BYLINE: Sure thing, Scott.SIMON: And how powerful are these new AI models?RUWITCH: Yeah. I've talked with a couple of people who've used related models, and they say they are really powerful. The AI model that kind of kicked all this off is called Mythos. It's from Anthropic. It's not public yet, but a small number of tech companies have been getting exclusive access to a preview version. That includes companies like Google, Microsoft. It also includes Cisco. I spoke with the head of security at Cisco, Anthony Grieco.ANTHONY GRIECO: AI is a game changer for security, period. The world is changing. Mythos has amped up that change.RUWITCH: He's talking about cybersecurity, of course. That's one of the things that Mythos was designed to be good at. And what it does is find vulnerabilities in software and help fix them. These are the kind of vulnerabilities that hackers might want to exploit. Grieco says his team had Mythos scan 1.8 billion lines of code across several coding languages, and he says it did that deftly.GRIECO: And allowed us to cover that amount of territory and amount of complexity in just eight weeks, which would have otherwise taken humans mixed with machines and tools eight-plus years to do.SIMON: I mean, John, eight years of work in just eight weeks is astounding, but it also creates some concern, doesn't it?RUWITCH: Yeah. These are double-edged swords, right? So good guys can use tools like this to find and fix problems. Bad guys could use them to find and exploit holes, to hijack networks or steal data, that kind of thing. In fact, that's happening actually already with widely available, less powerful AI tools. There's been an explosion in the number of AI-assisted cyberattacks, and, you know, fear of that getting supercharged could be what's behind the administration's shift from being pretty hands-off about AI to wanting more control.SIMON: So are AI models being seen now as a potential national security risk?RUWITCH: Yeah. It seems like the cutting-edge ones might be. I mean, Anthropic, in early June, put out two AI models that were based on Mythos. They were weaker. Now, they had guardrails built in so they couldn't do the most powerful cyber stuff. Within days, though, the Trump administration told Anthropic it knew of a way that those safeguards could be circumvented, and so it slapped an export ban on those models, basically, meaning no foreigners could use them, including Anthropic employees. So the company took them offline.Fast-forward to Friday, the Commerce Department partially lifted the export ban on one of those two models. According to a letter from the commerce secretary that NPR has seen, it's now allowing a small list of American companies, including their foreign staffers, to use the model. In a statement emailed to NPR, Anthropic said it was pleased with this change, and it'll continue to work with the government.SIMON: And is working with the government now a new expectation?RUWITCH: Yeah. It seems to be. OpenAI said just yesterday also that it had let the government vet a list of companies that would have access to its latest and greatest model. You know, the company said this was the strongest path to broader availability of the model in the coming weeks but that this kind of vetting should not be the norm long term.SIMON: You've been talking to cybersecurity experts. What do they see in the future for these tools?RUWITCH: Yeah. The tools are powerful. Some think these models will tilt the balance in their sort of arms race against hackers. Lee Klarich is with Palo Alto Networks, a cybersecurity company that's had access to some of these models. He says there's a wave now of companies like his finding and fixing vulnerabilities.LEE KLARICH: And then as we come down the other side of that as an industry, we'll actually go to a level where we are - have more secure software than we've had before because we're finding and fixing things before they ever get released in the first place.RUWITCH: That may be the case. It may be a little optimistic, but experts say, you know, this now AI-enabled cat-and-mouse game between cyberattackers and defenders is not ending anytime soon.SIMON: John Ruwitch. Thanks so much. And we want to note, Anthropic, Microsoft and Google are all financial supporters of NPR.
contentParagraphs.zh: 主持人斯科特·西蒙：美国政府对人工智能的控制达到了新的水平。本月早些时候，它禁止外国人使用 Anthropic 的两款最强大的新模型。它昨天部分撤销了禁令，但保留了对哪些公司可以访问的控制权。与此同时，Anthropic 的竞争对手 OpenAI 昨天表示，它已同意让政府筛选其最新型号的用户。 NPR 科技记者 John Ruwitch 加入我们。约翰，非常感谢您与我们在一起。约翰·鲁维奇连线：当然可以，斯科特。西蒙：这些新的人工智能模型有多强大？鲁维奇：是的。我和几个使用过相关模型的人交谈过，他们说这些模型真的很强大。启动这一切的人工智能模型被称为 Mythos。它来自人类。该版本尚未公开，但少数科技公司已经获得了预览版本的独家访问权。其中包括谷歌、微软等公司。其中还包括思科。我与思科安全主管 Anthony Grieco 进行了交谈。 ANTHONY GRIECO：人工智能是安全领域的游戏规则改变者。世界正在发生变化。 Mythos 加速了这一变化。RUWITCH：当然，他谈论的是网络安全。这是 Mythos 被设计为擅长的事情之一。它的作用是发现软件中的漏洞并帮助修复它们。这些是黑客可能想要利用的漏洞。 Grieco 说他的团队让 Mythos 扫描了多种编码语言的 18 亿行代码，他说它做得很熟练。 GRIECO：让我们在短短八周内覆盖了如此大的范围和如此复杂的程度，否则这需要人类与机器和工具混合八年多的时间才能完成。 SIMON：我的意思是，约翰，在短短八周内完成八年的工作是令人震惊的，但这也引起了一些担忧，不是吗？ RUWITCH：是的。这些是双刃剑，对吧？所以好人可以使用这样的工具来发现和解决问题。坏人可以利用它们来发现和利用漏洞、劫持网络或窃取数据等。事实上，这种情况已经在广泛使用但功能较弱的人工智能工具中发生了。人工智能辅助的网络攻击数量呈爆炸式增长，而且，你知道，对这种攻击的过度担心可能是政府从对人工智能相当不干涉转变为想要更多控制的原因。 西蒙：那么人工智能模型现在是否被视为潜在的国家安全风险？ RUWITCH：是的。看起来可能是最前沿的。我的意思是，Anthropic 在六月初推出了两个基于 Mythos 的人工智能模型。他们更弱。现在，他们内置了护栏，因此他们无法执行最强大的网络操作。然而，几天之内，特朗普政府告诉 Anthropic，它知道可以规避这些保障措施的方法，因此它对这些模型实施了出口禁令，基本上意味着任何外国人都不能使用它们，包括 Anthropic 员工。因此该公司将它们下线。快进到周五，商务部部分解除了对这两种型号之一的出口禁令。根据美国公共广播电台看到的一封来自商务部长的信，美国现在允许一小部分美国公司（包括其外籍员工）使用该模式。在通过电子邮件发送给 NPR 的一份声明中，Anthropic 表示对这一变化感到满意，并将继续与政府合作。 SIMON：现在与政府合作是一个新的期望吗？RUWITCH：是的。看来是的。 OpenAI 昨天还表示，它已经让政府审查了一份可以使用其最新、最好的模型的公司名单。你知道，该公司表示，这是未来几周内更广泛使用该模型的最有力途径，但这种审查不应该成为长期常态。 西蒙：你一直在与网络安全专家交谈。他们对这些工具的未来有何看法？RUWITCH：是的。这些工具很强大。一些人认为这些模型将在与黑客的军备竞赛中扭转平衡。 Lee Klarich 就职于 Palo Alto Networks，这是一家网络安全公司，可以访问其中一些模型。他说，现在有一波像他这样寻找和修复漏洞的公司。 LEE KLARICH：然后，当我们作为一个行业走到另一边时，我们实际上会达到我们现在的水平 - 拥有比以前更安全的软件，因为我们在东西发布之前就找到并修复了它们。 RUWITCH：情况可能是这样。这可能有点乐观，但专家表示，你知道，网络攻击者和防御者之间现在由人工智能支持的猫捉老鼠游戏不会很快结束。西蒙：约翰·鲁维奇（John Ruwitch）。非常感谢。我们要指出的是，Anthropic、微软和谷歌都是 NPR 的财务支持者。

articles.title: Trump threatens 100% tariff on European nations over tech tax
articles.summary: The US president says "Numerous European countries" have been discussing bringing in such a levy.
articles.titleZh: 特朗普威胁对欧洲国家征收100%的科技税关税
articles.summaryZh: 美国总统表示，“许多欧洲国家”一直在讨论征收此类税。

contentParagraphs.en: US president Donald Trump has vowed to impose a 100% import tariff on any European country that introduces a digital services tax on American technology giants. Writing on his Truth Social platform, Trump said "numerous European countries" had been discussing bringing in such a levy and some were close to doing so.He warned that the punitive penalties would be applied immediately and would completely "supersede" any existing bilateral trade agreements. While the post targets nations planning the "imminent implementation" of new levies, the precise implications for the UK were not immediately clear, given London has had such a tax in place since 2020."Please let this statement serve to represent that any Country that imposes such a Tax will immediately be met with a 100% TARIFF on any and all Goods sent to the United States of America," he wrote.Britain's 2% Digital Services Tax (DST) applies to major search engines, social media platforms, and online marketplaces with global revenues from their digital businesses exceeding £500 million, and total UK revenues surpassing £25 million.It impacts some of the largest US companies, including Apple, Google, Meta, and Amazon and raised more than £800 million in 2024–25, up from £678 million in 2023–24, according to the Treasury.In April, Trump said that the UK faced "a big tariff" for purportedly targeting major US companies with a tax."They think they're going to make an easy buck, that's why they've all taken advantage of our country", Trump said at the time.The Department for Business and Trade and the Treasury have been contacted for comment. Trump's threat of retaliation against European nations that may be planning to launch or revise their own such tax comes just days after the US and EU finalised a new trade deal, external.Michael Damianos, minister of energy, commerce and industry of the Republic of Cyprus, said at the time that "the EU can respond swiftly and proportionately when the deal is not respected or its interests are at stake".France, Italy and Spain also impose a digital services tax of 3% on large companies operating in their countries, and several other EU nations have implemented or proposed, external a similar tax, according to Tax Foundation, a nonprofit group focused on tax policy. Amazon earlier this year upped its fees on sellers, external citing such taxes.Trump has attempted to impose large tariffs on many countries since he became president again in 2025. The US Supreme Court in February struck down Trump's earlier attempt to impose a global tariff of 10%.Nevertheless, the US recently announced new tariffs of 10-12.5% on dozens of countries accounting for almost all its imports over claims that such countries are not doing enough to tackle forced labour.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国总统唐纳德·特朗普誓言将对任何对美国科技巨头征收数字服务税的欧洲国家征收100%的进口关税。特朗普在他的“真相社会”平台上写道，“许多欧洲国家”一直在讨论征收此类关税，有些国家已经接近这样做。他警告说，惩罚性处罚将立即实施，并将完全“取代”任何现有的双边贸易协定。虽然该帖子针对的是计划“即将实施”新征税的国家，但鉴于伦敦自 2020 年以来就开始征收此类税，因此对英国的确切影响尚不清楚。他写道：“请让此声明代表任何征收此类税的国家将立即对发送到美利坚合众国的任何及所有商品征收 100% 的关税。”英国 2% 的数字服务税 (DST) 适用于主要搜索引擎，数字业务全球收入超过 5 亿英镑，英国总收入超过 2500 万英镑的社交媒体平台和在线市场。根据财政部的数据，它影响了一些最大的美国公司，包括苹果、谷歌、Meta 和亚马逊，并在 2024-25 年筹集了超过 8 亿英镑，高于 2023-24 年的 6.78 亿英镑。4 月，特朗普表示，英国因据称针对美国主要公司的特朗普当时表示，“他们认为自己可以轻松赚钱，这就是为什么他们都在利用我们国家的好处”。我们已联系商业贸易部和财政部征求评论。就在美国和欧盟敲定一项新的对外贸易协议几天后，特朗普威胁要对可能计划启动或修改本国此类税收的欧洲国家进行报复。塞浦路斯共和国能源、商业和工业部长迈克尔·达米亚诺斯当时表示，“当协议不被遵守或其利益受到威胁时，欧盟可以迅速、相应地做出反应”。法国、意大利和西班牙还对在其境内运营的大型公司征收 3% 的数字服务税。据专注于税收政策的非营利组织税务基金会称，一些国家和其他几个欧盟国家已经实施或提议征收类似的税收。亚马逊今年早些时候提高了对卖家的收费，对外以此类税收为由。特朗普自2025年再次就任总统以来，一直试图对许多国家征收高额关税。美国最高法院在2月驳回了特朗普此前提出的征收10%全球关税的企图。尽管如此，美国最近宣布对几乎占其所有进口产品的数十个国家征收10-12.5%的新关税，理由是这些国家在解决强迫劳动问题上做得不够。

articles.title: Could legal 'personhood' help save Lough Neagh?
articles.summary: Rights of Nature could give Lough Neagh the same status as a person, entitled to the same protections.
articles.titleZh: 法律“人格”能否帮助拯救内湖？
articles.summaryZh: 自然权利可以赋予内湖与人相同的地位，有权获得相同的保护。

contentParagraphs.en: Salazar-Ortuño began work with the backing of more than 600,000 people in Murcia, who signed a legal petition calling for protection."We went to the parliament in order to change the existing anthropocentric law into an ecocentric law," Salazar-Ortuño said."Then we decided to propose our parliament with 600,000 signatures that the Mar Menor needs to have rights and legal personhood."A tutorship, or guardianship, was created, which meant that anybody could go to court on the lagoon's behalf."They are represented by many stakeholders around the lagoon - fishermen, farmers, citizens, neighbours, environmental groups."The Mar Menor has a tax number, a private account, and then when one citizen or the tutorship asks for restoration against any of the farmers, against any of the polluters of the Mar Menor, the restoration goes directly to the Mar Menor."And it has already had an impact."There is a big pressure in the environmental ministry - they have invested more than €600 million (£517.5m) by restoring of the Mar Menor," Salazar-Ortuño said."So it's a big impact after the mobilisation."
