articles.title: FIFA World Cup: Round of 32 bracket, schedule, predictions, Iran’s exit
articles.summary: Knockout matches begin with South Africa vs Canada as Iran exit, Africa make history, Messi-Ronaldo final hopes rise.
articles.titleZh: FIFA 世界杯：32 强赛、赛程、预测、伊朗出局
articles.summaryZh: 淘汰赛从南非对阵加拿大开始，伊朗出局，非洲创造历史，梅西-罗纳尔多决赛的希望上升。

contentParagraphs.en: Knockout matches begin with South Africa vs Canada as Iran exit, Africa make history and hopes for Messi-Ronaldo final rise.The knockout stage of the 2026 FIFA World Cup begins on Sunday with South Africa taking on Canada in the first round of 32 tie.With the group stage complete, the full knockout bracket is now set. Nine African nations have reached the round of 32, Iran were eliminated after Algeria’s late qualification and the draw has left the door open to a Lionel Messi vs Cristiano Ronaldo final.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4Who’s playing whom in the World Cup 2026 last-32 knockout phase?list 2 of 4DR Congo fans celebrate reaching World Cup knockout stagelist 3 of 4South Africa and Canada open World Cup round of 32 – all to knowlist 4 of 4VIDEO: World Cup Day 17: Messi breaks record; DR Congo and Algeria advanceend of listHere’s the full round of 32 schedule, the South Africa vs Canada prediction and the latest World Cup news:What is Sunday’s schedule? South Africa vs Canada at Los Angeles Stadium, California, in the United States at noon (19:00 GMT). What are the predictions for South Africa vs Canada?This will be only the second meeting between South Africa and Canada. The sides’ only previous encounter ended in a 2-0 win for Bafana Bafana in a friendly in Durban in 2007.Canada, however, will be looking to end another unwanted record. They have lost both of their previous competitive matches against African opposition, falling 2-0 to Cameroon at the 2001 Confederations Cup before a 2-1 defeat to Morocco at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar.Despite that history, the Opta supercomputer shows Canada as the clear favourites. They won 55 percent of 25,000 pre-match simulations while South Africa triumphed in 20 percent. The remaining 24.9 percent of its calculations ended level after 90 minutes, which would send the tie to extra time and potentially penalties.Overall, Canada are given a 67.8 percent chance of reaching the quarterfinals, compared with 32.2 percent for South Africa.When and where will the other knockout matches be played?Monday Brazil vs Japan: (noon/17:00 GMT) at Houston Stadium, Texas, in the US Germany vs Paraguay: (4:30pm/20:30 GMT) at Boston Stadium, Massachusetts, in the US Netherlands vs Morocco: (7pm/01:00 GMT on Tuesday) at Monterrey Stadium in Mexico Tuesday Ivory Coast vs Norway (noon/17:00 GMT) at Dallas Stadium, Texas, in the US France vs Sweden (5pm/21:00 GMT) at New York/New Jersey Stadium in the US Mexico vs Ecuador (7pm/02:00 GMT on Wednesday) at Mexico City Stadium in Mexico Wednesday England vs Democratic Republic of the Congo (noon/16:00 GMT) at Atlanta Stadium, Georgia, in the US Belgium vs Senegal (1pm/20:00 GMT) at Seattle Stadium in the US state of Washington USA vs Bosnia and Herzegovina (5pm/00:00 GMT on Thursday) at San Francisco Bay Area Stadium, California, in the US Thursday Spain vs Austria (noon/19:00 GMT) at Los Angeles Stadium Portugal vs Croatia (7pm/23:00 GMT) at Toronto Stadium, Ontario, Canada Switzerland vs Algeria (8pm/03:00 GMT on Friday) at BC Place Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada Friday Australia vs Egypt (1pm/18:00 GMT) at Dallas Stadium Argentina vs Cape Verde (6pm/22:00 GMT) at Miami Stadium, Florida, in the US Colombia vs Ghana (8:30pm/01:30 GMT on Saturday) at Kansas City Stadium, Missouri, in the US What else is happening?Is Iran eliminated from the 2026 World Cup?Yes. Iran have been eliminated from the 2026 FIFA World Cup.Team Melli’s hopes depended on the final Group J match on Saturday between Austria and Algeria. A draw was the only result that could have knocked Iran out, and the 3-3 score meant that Algeria moved above Iran in the ranking of third-placed teams.Algeria finished third in Group J on four points, enough to take the final available place among the eight best third-placed teams, which advanced to the round of 32.Iran, who had been holding the last qualifying spot, were, therefore, eliminated in the group stage.Africa set a new World Cup recordAfrican teams have enjoyed their best ever men’s World Cup campaign.A record 10 African nations qualified for the expanded 2026 tournament, and nine have reached the round of 32, the most from the continent in a single World Cup.Those who qualified are: Algeria, Cape Verde, DR Congo, Egypt, Ghana, Ivory Coast, Morocco, Senegal and South Africa.Tunisia are the only African side eliminated so far.The success builds on Africa’s growing influence on the world stage after Morocco’s historic run to the 2022 semifinals. With more nations now consistently challenging football’s traditional powers, the continent is enjoying its strongest World Cup showing yet.Lionel Messi, then of PSG, and Cristiano Ronaldo, part of an exhibition Riyadh XI side, last played each other in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia on January 19, 2023 [EPA-EFE]Messi vs Ronaldo final?A potential knockout clash between superstars Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo remains a possibility – but only if both captains lead their teams all the way to the World Cup 2026 final.With the round of 32 bracket now confirmed, Argentina and Portugal are on opposite sides of the draw, ruling out the quarterfinal meeting many fans had anticipated. That means football’s two greatest modern rivals can face each other only if both reach the final on July 19.The bracket has sparked widespread reaction on social media, where fans have been sharing predictions, memes and hopeful scenarios for one last meeting between the two icons, who are both playing in their sixth World Cup.
contentParagraphs.zh: 淘汰赛从南非队对阵加拿大队开始，伊朗队出局，非洲队创造历史，希望梅西和罗纳尔多在决赛中崛起。2026 年 FIFA 世界杯淘汰赛阶段将于周日开始，南非队在 32 强第一轮比赛中对阵加拿大队。随着小组赛的结束，完整的淘汰赛分组现已确定。九个非洲国家已进入 32 强，伊朗在阿尔及利亚资格赛后期被淘汰，抽签为莱昂内尔·梅西 (Lionel Messi) 与克里斯蒂亚诺·罗纳尔多 (Cristiano Ronaldo) 的决赛敞开了大门。推荐故事列表 4 项列表 1 of 4 谁将在 2026 年世界杯最后 32 场淘汰赛阶段对阵谁？列表 2 4DR 刚果球迷庆祝进入世界杯淘汰赛阶段列表 3 of 4南非和加拿大开启世界杯 32 强赛– 4VIDEO 的全部知识列表 4：世界杯第 17 天：梅西打破纪录；刚果民主共和国和阿尔及利亚晋级名单以下是32强赛的完整赛程、南非对加拿大的预测以及最新的世界杯新闻：周日的赛程是什么？中午（格林尼治标准时间 19:00）南非队对阵加拿大队在美国加利福尼亚州洛杉矶体育场举行。南非对阵加拿大的预测是什么？这只是南非和加拿大之间的第二次比赛。双方此前唯一一次交手是 2007 年德班的一场友谊赛，巴法纳·巴法纳 (Bafana Bafana) 2-0 获胜。然而，加拿大队将寻求结束另一项不受欢迎的纪录。他们之前两场对阵非洲对手的正式比赛都输了，2001 年联合会杯上以 2-0 输给了喀麦隆，然后在 2022 年卡塔尔世界杯上以 2-1 输给了摩洛哥。尽管有这样的历史，Opta 超级计算机显示加拿大是明显的热门。在 25,000 次赛前模拟中，他们的胜率为 55%，而南非队的胜率为 20%。剩余 24.9% 的计算在 90 分钟后结束，这将使平局进入加时赛并可能受到处罚。总体而言，加拿大进入四分之一决赛的机会为 67.8%，而南非为 32.2%。其他淘汰赛将在何时何地进行？周一巴西 vs 日本：（中午/17:00 GMT）在美国德克萨斯州休斯顿体育场德国 vs 巴拉圭： （下午 4:30/20:30 GMT）在美国马萨诸塞州波士顿体育场荷兰 vs 摩洛哥：（周二 7pm/01:00 GMT）在墨西哥蒙特雷体育场周二科特迪瓦 vs 挪威（中午/17:00 GMT）在美国德克萨斯州达拉斯体育场法国 vs 瑞典（5pm/21:00 GMT）在美国纽约/新泽西体育场墨西哥 vs 厄瓜多尔（7pm/02:00 GMT）星期三）在墨西哥墨西哥城体育场举行 星期三 英格兰对阵刚果民主共和国（中午/16:00 GMT）在美国佐治亚州亚特兰大体育场举行 比利时对阵塞内加尔（下午 1 点/20:00 GMT）在美国华盛顿州西雅图体育场举行 美国对阵波斯尼亚和黑塞哥维那（星期四下午 5 点/00:00 GMT）在美国加利福尼亚州旧金山湾区体育场举行 星期四西班牙对阵奥地利（中午/19:00 GMT）洛杉矶体育场 葡萄牙 vs 克罗地亚 (7pm/23:00 GMT) 加拿大安大略省多伦多体育场 瑞士 vs 阿尔及利亚 (周五 8pm/03:00 GMT) 加拿大不列颠哥伦比亚省温哥华 BC Place 周五 澳大利亚 vs 埃及 (1pm/18:00 GMT) 达拉斯体育场 阿根廷 vs 佛得角 (6pm/22:00 GMT) 美国佛罗里达州迈阿密体育场 哥伦比亚 vs 加纳 (8:30pm/01:30 GMT)星期六）在美国密苏里州堪萨斯城体育场发生了什么事？伊朗队是否被淘汰出 2026 年世界杯？是的。伊朗队已被淘汰出 2026 年 FIFA 世界杯。梅利队的希望取决于周六奥地利队与阿尔及利亚队之间的 J 组最后一场比赛。平局是唯一能够淘汰伊朗队的结果，而3-3的比分意味着阿尔及利亚队在第三名球队的排名上超越了伊朗队。阿尔及利亚队积四分在J组获得第三名，足以在八支成绩最好的第三名球队中获得最后的名额，晋级32强。原本持有最后一个出线名额的伊朗队因此在小组赛中被淘汰。非洲队创下了世界杯的新纪录。有史以来最好的男子世界杯征程。创纪录的 10 个非洲国家获得了 2026 年扩大赛的参赛资格，其中 9 个国家进入了 32 强，这是单届世界杯上来自非洲大陆最多的国家。晋级的国家包括：阿尔及利亚、佛得角、刚果民主共和国、埃及、加纳、科特迪瓦、摩洛哥、塞内加尔和南非。突尼斯是迄今为止唯一一支被淘汰的非洲球队。这一成功建立在非洲在世界舞台上日益增长的影响力之上。摩洛哥历史性地闯入 2022 年半决赛。随着越来越多的国家不断挑战足球传统强国，非洲大陆正在享受迄今为止最强劲的世界杯表现。当时的巴黎圣日耳曼队的莱昂内尔·梅西和克里斯蒂亚诺·罗纳尔多，利雅得十一队的一部分，最后一次交锋是2023年1月19日在沙特阿拉伯利雅得[EPA-EFE]梅西对罗纳尔多决赛？超级巨星莱昂内尔·梅西和克里斯蒂亚诺·罗纳尔多之间潜在的淘汰赛仍然有可能 - 但只有两位队长带领球队一路杀入2026年世界杯决赛。随着32强淘汰赛的确定，阿根廷和葡萄牙在抽签中处于对立面，排除了许多球迷期待的四分之一决赛相遇。这意味着这两个现代足坛最伟大的对手只有在 7 月 19 日进入决赛后才能面对面。这场比赛在社交媒体上引起了广泛反响，球迷们纷纷分享对这两位标志性人物最后一次交锋的预测、表情包和充满希望的场景，他们都是第六次参加世界杯。

articles.title: Screen time can damage under-twos’ development, landmark study suggests
articles.summary:
Exclusive: Researchers call for urgent investigation of risks to babies of tablets, smartphones and other digital devices
Screen time for babies and toddlers under the age of two has been linked with long-term negative effects on health and quality of life and should be avoided,
articles.titleZh: 具有里程碑意义的研究表明，屏幕时间会损害两岁以下儿童的发育
articles.summaryZh:
独家：研究人员呼吁紧急调查平板电脑、智能手机和其他数字设备对婴儿的风险
两岁以下婴儿和幼儿的屏幕时间与对健康和生活质量的长期负面影响有关，应该避免，

contentParagraphs.en: Screen time for babies and toddlers under the age of two has been linked with long-term negative effects on health and quality of life and should be avoided, according to a landmark study.It warns that using screens during that period may lead to wide-ranging developmental concerns and calls for further urgent investigation of the risks smartphones, tablets and other digital devices pose to infants.With the focus on teenagers’ digital habits and government plans to ban under-16s from social media, researchers are concerned about a “baby blind spot” in policy at a time when screen use has become embedded in everyday parenting.Rafe Clayton, a senior lecturer in media and communication at the University of Leeds, who co-led the research, said parents – lacking guidance on their own screen use – were “inadvertently teaching children and babies to develop unhealthy habits and relationships with screen devices”.“This has to change,” he said.The study, described as the most comprehensive review yet of all available global research on the subject, calls on the government to reconsider its recently published guidance on screen time for under-fives.That recommends avoiding screen time for the under-twos but caveats the advice by adding, “other than shared activities that encourage bonding, interaction and conversation”.The new study, however, lays bare a wide range of potential harms associated with screen time for babies, including reduced opportunities to bond with parents and caregivers, less time for physical play with other children and limited language development.It says screen use at such a young age may increase overstimulation and difficulty sleeping, and have implications for eye health and childhood obesity. There are also concerns that infants are turning to digital devices for comfort and soothing, rather than to a parent.The review, conducted by researchers from four UK universities known as the Action on Digital Device Immersive Conditions Team, did not establish causal links between screen use and specific developmental conditions. However, it was emphatic that “no under-twos should receive regular intentional screen time. Passive exposure is societally unavoidable, so adding deliberate use compounds risk without any meaningful benefit.”It recommends that any official guidance that points under-twos towards regular “shared screen time, screen time for learning, screen time for communication and/or screen time for children experiencing disabilities/learning difficulties, should be reconsidered, since it may be misinterpreted by parents and caregivers to indicate safety or even encouragement.“This could potentially lead caregivers to believe that screen time for the under-twos is without developmental harm, which may result in an exacerbation of developmental delays and isolating behaviours for those who are already at greater risk.”As a result of their findings, the research team – from the universities of Leeds, Leeds Trinity, Loughborough and Aston – are calling for a “baby screen-time risk assessment” to help services provide targeted support for families where developmental vulnerabilities may be emerging.Carmen Clayton, professor of family and cultural dynamics at Leeds Trinity University and co-lead of the research, said: “The government must consider how to engage with families better about problematic screen use, whilst being sensitive to the fear of judgment that many parents face when opening up about such issues.”Andrea Leadsom, a former Conservative minister and founder of the 1,001 Critical Days Foundation, said: “This landmark review is a wake-up call. The evidence increasingly suggests that screens offer limited benefits for babies and may carry significant risks during the first 1,001 days, the most important period of human development.”She said parents should not be blamed for a problem they did not create. “The responsibility cannot rest solely on their shoulders. That is why every family should have access to a Best Start family hub, where they could access trusted advice and practical help during their baby’s earliest years.”Leadsom said technology companies should play their part. “Parents should not be presented with content that is labelled or promoted as suitable for babies when the evidence points to the contrary.”The children’s commissioner for England, Rachel de Souza, who helped draw up the government guidance, said it was intended to support – not replace – parental judgment.“For children under two, the recommendation to avoid screen time is clear, but acknowledges the realities of our world today and that some shared screen use in a limited number of circumstances, such as video-calling relatives or supported learning, is perfectly normal.”A Department for Education spokesperson said: “We’re proud of our first-of-its-kind screen time guidance for parents of under-fives, which provides clear, trusted support on an issue we know can be challenging for families.”
contentParagraphs.zh: 一项具有里程碑意义的研究表明，两岁以下婴儿和幼儿的屏幕时间与对健康和生活质量的长期负面影响有关，应该避免。该研究警告说，在此期间使用屏幕可能会导致广泛的发育问题，并呼吁进一步紧急调查智能手机、平板电脑和其他数字设备对婴儿造成的风险。随着对青少年数字习惯的关注以及政府计划禁止 16 岁以下儿童使用社交媒体，研究人员担心政策中的“婴儿盲点”这项研究的共同领导者、利兹大学媒体和传播学高级讲师拉夫·克莱顿 (Rafe Clayton) 表示，父母缺乏对自己屏幕使用的指导，“无意中教会了孩子和婴儿养成不健康的习惯以及与屏幕设备的关系”。他说，“这种情况必须改变。”这项研究被描述为迄今为止对这一主题的所有可用全球研究的最全面的回顾，呼吁政府重新考虑其最近发表的研究报告。关于五岁以下儿童的屏幕时间指南。该指南建议两岁以下儿童避免使用屏幕时间，但补充道，“除了鼓励亲密、互动和对话的共同活动之外”。然而，这项新研究揭示了婴儿使用屏幕时间带来的一系列潜在危害，包括减少与父母和看护者建立联系的机会、减少与其他孩子一起玩耍的时间以及限制语言发展。报告称，在这么小的年龄使用屏幕可能会增加过度刺激和睡眠困难，并对眼睛健康和儿童期产生影响肥胖。还有人担心婴儿会转向数字设备来寻求舒适和安抚，而不是向父母求助。这项审查由来自四所英国大学的研究人员进行，名为“数字设备沉浸条件行动小组”，并未建立屏幕使用与特定发育条件之间的因果关系。然而，它强调，“两岁以下儿童不应定期有意识地接触屏幕时间。被动接触在社会上是不可避免的，因此增加故意使用屏幕时间会增加风险，而没有任何有意义的好处。”它建议，任何针对两岁以下儿童定期“共享屏幕时间、学习屏幕时间、交流屏幕时间和/或有残疾/学习困难的儿童的屏幕时间”的官方指南都应该重新考虑，因为它可能会被父母和照顾者误解为表明安全或“这可能会导致护理人员相信两岁以下儿童的屏幕时间不会对发育造成损害，这可能会导致发育迟缓加剧，并导致那些已经面临更大风险的人孤立行为。”根据他们的发现，来自利兹大学、利兹三一大学、拉夫堡大学和阿斯顿大学的研究小组呼吁进行“婴儿屏幕时间风险评估”，以帮助服务机构为发育障碍的家庭提供有针对性的支持。利兹三一大学家庭与文化动力学教授、该研究的共同负责人卡门·克莱顿 (Carmen Clayton) 表示：“政府必须考虑如何与家庭更好地沟通有关屏幕使用问题的问题，同时要对许多家长在公开此类问题时面临的评判恐惧保持敏感。” 前保守党部长、1001 个关键日基金会创始人安德里亚·利德索姆 (Andrea Leadsom) 表示：“这一具有里程碑意义的审查是一记警钟。越来越多的证据表明，屏幕对婴儿的好处有限，而且在人类发育最重要的最初 1001 天里可能会带来重大风险。”她说，父母不应该因为他们没有造成的问题而受到指责。“责任不能仅仅落在他们的肩上。这就是为什么每个家庭都应该有机会使用 Best Start 家庭中心，在那里他们可以在宝宝最早的时候获得值得信赖的建议和实际帮助。”Leadsom 表示，科技公司应该发挥自己的作用。“当证据表明相反时，父母不应该看到被标记或宣传为适合婴儿的内容。”帮助起草政府指南的英格兰儿童事务专员 Rachel de Souza 表示，该指南的目的是支持而不是取代父母的判断。“对于两岁以下的儿童，该建议避免使用屏幕时间的做法很明确，但也承认当今世界的现实，在有限的情况下使用共享屏幕，例如与亲戚视频通话或支持学习，是完全正常的。”教育部发言人表示：“我们为我们为五岁以下儿童家长提供的首个屏幕时间指南感到自豪，该指南为我们知道对家庭可能具有挑战性的问题提供了明确、值得信赖的支持。”

articles.title: Could you handle a 20-plus hour flight? This airline is banking on it
articles.summary: Some analysts say the first non-stop London to Sydney flight is a major milestone - but would you buy a ticket?
articles.titleZh: 你能承受20多个小时的飞行吗？这家航空公司指望它
articles.summaryZh: 一些分析人士表示，首个伦敦直飞悉尼的航班是一个重要的里程碑，但您会买机票吗？

contentParagraphs.en: Could you handle a 20-plus hour flight? This airline is banking on itImage source, QantasImage caption, The non-stop London to Sydney flight would enter new frontiers in aviationByRachel ClunOsmond ChiaBusiness reporters and Kris BramwellOn a stage at the Airbus headquarters in Toulouse, the chief executive of Australian airline Qantas declares: "The tyranny of distance has finally been conquered".Vanessa Hudson was in the French city last week to announce the world's first 20-plus hour flight route.The airline first flew what it named the Kangaroo route between London and Sydney in 1947. At the time, it was an odyssey spanning seven stops and four days.Those stops have been gradually reduced, with Qantas now stopping only once, in Singapore, on the way through.But 80 years after that 1940s venture, the first non-stop flight between the two cities is set to take off from October 2027.Using specially designed ultra-long-haul Airbus planes, Qantas expects to shave about four hours off the current journey time. It is expected to last around 22 hours.The much anticipated - and delayed - breakthrough comes after a turbulent few years in the airline's history, and bosses are banking on customers embracing the premium but marathon flight."We feel really confident that this is going to be a success," Hudson tells the BBC.Some analysts say it is a major milestone in aviation history. But is it really what people want?One stop too far in price - or not?Qantas has overcome some challenges to get this far - and still faces others.The flight will save money on landing fees by eliminating a stop, but Hudson admits the longer flight has a higher relative fuel bill.There are also fewer seats, nearly half of which (40%) will be premium economy, business, or first class.To counter the increased risk of issues such as deep vein thrombosis which can occur from flying for such long periods, Qantas has increased the legroom in economy and also created a dedicated "wellness" space where passengers can follow stretching exercises on a screen and have a little more room to move about.Hudson points to the success of the Perth to London route, saying "customers have been prepared to pay a premium" for that service.Australian travel agent Karis Heemskerk is among the fans of spending more time on one plane to get to their destination faster.Image source, Karis HeemskerkImage caption, Karis Heemskerk is keen to avoid a stop on long-haul flightsThe 41-year-old has taken the roughly 18-hour flight from Perth to London a couple of times, including with her husband and two children, and says being able to fly direct is "amazing" and an efficient use of time."I think the direct flights cut time and there is no risk of missed connections and the stress of your luggage being lost," she tells the BBC."Cons are that it can be gruelling and it is a long time for some individuals to be confined to a cabin. [But] overall, I'm a big fan of the direct flights."However, some frequent fliers such as Tom Gill are less interested. The 33-year-old cultural consultant, who is originally from London but lives in Melbourne, travels at least once a year to London plus other trips to Europe."I don't mind an airport stopover at all: the idea of sitting in a plane for 20, 21 hours non-stop would be quite unbearable for me," he says.Image source, Tom GillImage caption, Tom Gill is put off by the expected cost of the London-Sydney flightFor Gill, the main factor is cost. Given the new route is expected to cost about 20% more than its current Sydney to London offering with a stopover, he doesn't think it will be a flight he'll catch anytime soon."To be clear, I'd try anything once. If it was cheaper I would definitely consider it."Research from ABTA suggests an increase in the number of people who travelled from the UK to Australia in the past year, particularly among 18-24-year-olds."Australia is for many of us a bucket list destination," the UK travel industry body tells us.But Bryan Terry, managing director of Alton Aviation Consultancy says demand for this sort of service is narrow - posing a risk for the airline."Qantas is targeting premium and time-sensitive travellers willing to pay a meaningful premium to avoid a Dubai, Singapore, or Los Angeles connection," he says.Singapore Airlines currently has the world's longest flight - between Singapore and New York - and Terry notes the route proves people are willing to pay "significantly more" to eliminate a stopover.The last frontierImage source, QantasImage caption, The Airbus A350-1000, seen here on a test flight, comes with an extra fuel tankTerry says Qantas is conquering "one of the last frontiers in commercial aviation"."Every generation of aircraft has chipped away at Australia's isolation, but a non-stop Sydney to London or New York has always been just out of reach," he adds.It's an effort that has been years in the making, but which has also faced several setbacks and delays.The programme to develop the non-stop London to Sydney route, dubbed Project Sunrise, was launched in 2017 - around the same time as the first direct London to Perth flights were announced. Previous announcements about the route launching have stalled, but the project now seems to be coming to fruition with the first of 12 Airbus A350-1000 aircraft being delivered to Qantas in April 2026.Figure caption, Watch: Would Australians choose to take a 22-hour non-stop flight?These come with an extra fuel tank to help increase the plane's flying time to 22 hours, with cabin lighting and meal times optimised to minimise jetlag on arrival.Airbus chief test pilot Malcolm Ridley says it has taken a relatively modest engineering change to adapt the aircraft for ultra-long-haul flights. While the first 12 aircraft must be delivered to Qantas before other airlines can buy them, he says there has already been some informal interest in the modified planes from competitors."When the aircraft goes into service and people can see
contentParagraphs.zh: 你能承受20多个小时的飞行吗？这家航空公司指望着它 图片来源，澳洲航空 图片说明，伦敦飞往悉尼的直飞航班将进入航空业的新领域 作者：Rachel ClunOsmond Chia 商业记者和 Kris Bramwell 在图卢兹空客总部的讲台上，澳大利亚航空公司澳洲航空首席执行官宣称：“距离的暴政终于被征服了”。 Vanessa Hudson 上周在法国城市宣布了世界上第一个 20 多个小时的航班该航空公司于 1947 年首次在伦敦和悉尼之间开通了名为“袋鼠”的航线。当时，这是一次跨越七站、四天的旅程。这些经停点已逐渐减少，澳洲航空现在只在途经新加坡的途中经停一次。但在 1940 年代的冒险 80 年后，两个城市之间的首个直飞航班将于 2027 年 10 月起飞。使用专门设计的超长途航班空客飞机、澳洲航空预计将目前的飞行时间缩短约四个小时。预计飞行时间约为 22 小时。在该航空公司经历了几年动荡的历史之后，这项备受期待但被推迟的突破出现了，老板们寄希望于客户接受优质但马拉松式的航班。“我们非常有信心这将取得成功，”哈德森告诉 BBC。一些分析师表示，这是航空历史上的一个重要里程碑。但这真的是人们想要的吗？一站的价格太远了——或者不是？澳航克服了一些挑战才走到这一步，但仍然面临着其他挑战。该航班通过取消中转可以节省着陆费，但哈德森承认，较长的航班相对燃油费用更高。座位也较少，其中近一半（40%）将是豪华经济舱、商务舱或头等舱。为了应对因长时间飞行而可能出现的深静脉血栓等问题的风险增加，澳航增加了经济舱的腿部空间，还创建了一个专门的“健康”空间，乘客可以在屏幕上进行伸展运动，并有更多的活动空间。哈德森指出珀斯至伦敦航线的成功，称“客户已经准备好为这项服务支付额外费用”。澳大利亚旅行社卡里斯·亨斯科克(Karis Heemskerk)是在一架飞机上花更多时间以更快地到达目的地的粉丝之一。图片来源，卡里斯·亨斯科克(Karis Heemskerk)这位 41 岁的女士曾多次乘坐从珀斯飞往伦敦的大约 18 小时的航班，其中包括与丈夫和两个孩子，她表示能够直飞是“令人惊奇的”，而且可以有效地利用时间。“我认为直飞航班节省了时间，而且不存在错过转机的风险和行李丢失的压力，”她告诉 BBC。 [但]总体而言，我非常喜欢直飞航班。”然而，汤姆·吉尔等一些经常乘坐飞机的人对此不太感兴趣。这位 33 岁的文化顾问来自伦敦，但住在墨尔本，每年至少去伦敦一次，还去其他欧洲旅行。“我根本不介意在机场中途停留：在飞机上不间断地坐 20、21 个小时的想法对我来说是难以忍受的，”他说。因素是成本。鉴于新航线的费用预计比目前的悉尼至伦敦中途停留航线高出约 20%，他认为自己不会很快搭乘这条航班。“需要明确的是，我什么都愿意尝试一次。如果更便宜，我肯定会考虑它。”ABTA 的研究表明，过去一年从英国飞往澳大利亚的人数有所增加，尤其是 18-24 岁的人。“澳大利亚对我们许多人来说都是一个机会。”英国旅游业机构告诉我们，“列出目的地”。但奥尔顿航空咨询公司董事总经理布莱恩·特里表示，这种服务的需求范围很窄，这给航空公司带来了风险。“澳洲航空的目标客户是愿意支付高额费用以避免迪拜、新加坡或洛杉矶转机的优质且对时间敏感的旅客。”他说。新加坡航空目前拥有世界上最长的航班，往返于新加坡和纽约之间，特里指出，这条航线证明人们愿意“花大钱”最后的前沿 图片来源，澳洲航空 图片说明，在试飞中看到的空客 A350-1000 配备了一个额外的油箱。特里表示，澳洲航空正在征服“商业航空的最后前沿之一”。“每一代飞机都在逐渐消除澳大利亚的孤立状态，但从悉尼直飞伦敦或纽约一直是遥不可及的，”他补充道。开发伦敦至悉尼直飞航线的计划被称为“日出计划”，于 2017 年启动，大约与首条伦敦至珀斯直飞航班宣布的同时。此前有关该航线启动的公告已陷入停滞，但该项目现在似乎即将取得成果，12 架空客 A350-1000 飞机中的第一架将于 2026 年 4 月交付给澳洲航空。图标题，观看：澳大利亚人会选择乘坐 22 小时直飞航班吗？这些飞机配有额外的油箱，有助于将飞机的飞行时间延长至 22 小时，并优化机舱照明和用餐时间，以最大限度地减少抵达时的时差反应。空客首席测试飞行员马尔科姆·里德利表示，他们对飞机进行了相对适度的工程改造，以适应超长途飞行。虽然前 12 架飞机必须先交付给澳洲航空，然后其他航空公司才能购买，但他表示，竞争对手已经对改装后的飞机产生了一些非正式的兴趣。“当飞机投入使用时，人们可以看到

articles.title: Critical fire weather complicates firefighting efforts in massive Utah wildfire
articles.summary: Firefighters working on the nation's largest current wildfire, burning in southern Utah, are being challenged by historic weather conditions. Extreme wildfire behavior is expected to continue through the weekend.
articles.titleZh: 严重的火灾天气使犹他州大规模野火的消防工作变得复杂
articles.summaryZh: 正在犹他州南部燃烧的美国目前最大的野火的消防队员正面临着历史性天气条件的挑战。极端的野火行为预计将持续整个周末。

contentParagraphs.en: The sun sets over the Cottonwood Fire near Marysvale, Utah, on Friday, June 26, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 6 月 26 日星期五，太阳落在犹他州马里斯维尔附近的卡顿伍德大火上。

contentParagraphs.en: Firefighters and residents in the Great Basin and Southwest are bracing for extreme wildfire conditions through the weekend.
contentParagraphs.zh: 大盆地和西南部的消防队员和居民正在为整个周末的极端野火情况做好准备。

contentParagraphs.en: The National Weather Service in Salt Lake City issued a rare "particularly dangerous situation" red flag warning in parts of Utah, Friday, for the first time in the office's history, due to a volatile combination of high winds, temperatures and low humidities.
contentParagraphs.zh: 由于大风、气温和低湿度的不稳定组合，盐湖城国家气象局周五在犹他州部分地区发布了罕见的“特别危险情况”红旗警告，这是该局历史上首次。

contentParagraphs.en: Critical fire weather conditions are expected to persist into Sunday, complicating efforts to contain the largest wildfire currently burning in the U.S., the Cottonwood Fire, which is burning in a sparsely populated part of southern Utah.
contentParagraphs.zh: 预计严重的火灾天气条件将持续到周日，这使得遏制美国目前最大的野火——卡顿伍德火灾——在犹他州南部人口稀少的地区燃烧的工作变得更加复杂。

contentParagraphs.en: "Our biggest challenge right now is that we have single digit humidities and the wind gusts are around 45 miles per hour," said Alyssa Mason, a spokesperson assigned to the fire. "That's on top of fuel moistures between 2 and 8 percent."
contentParagraphs.zh: 负责火灾的发言人阿丽莎·梅森 (Alyssa Mason) 表示：“我们目前面临的最大挑战是湿度为个位数，阵风约为每小时 45 英里。” “这是在燃料湿度为 2% 到 8% 的情况下计算的。”

contentParagraphs.en: Much of Utah, Nevada, Colorado and other states in the Intermountain West are experiencing widespread drought conditions after an abnormally dry winter. Surveyors documented their lowest snow levels on record in parts of the Rocky Mountains this winter. Utah's snowpack, which provides the state with much of its water as it melts, peaked three weeks earlier than normal and was also the lowest on record, according to the state's division of water resources.
contentParagraphs.zh: 犹他州、内华达州、科罗拉多州和西部山间其他州的大部分地区在经历了异常干燥的冬季后正经历大范围的干旱。测量员记录了今年冬天落基山脉部分地区有记录以来的最低降雪量。根据该州水资源部门的数据，犹他州的积雪在融化时为该州提供了大部分水，其峰值比正常情况提前了三周，也是有记录以来的最低水平。

contentParagraphs.en: On Friday afternoon, the combustible conditions combined with high winds forced the incident managers on the Cottonwood Fire to temporarily "pull guys off the line," Mason said. Helicopters and other firefighting aircraft were also grounded due to high winds.
contentParagraphs.zh: 梅森说，周五下午，易燃条件加上大风，迫使卡顿伍德火灾的事故管理人员暂时“把人员从火线上拉下来”。直升机和其他消防飞机也因大风而停飞。

contentParagraphs.en: The fire, which has burned an area larger than the size of Salt Lake City, remains completely uncontained.
contentParagraphs.zh: 大火烧毁的面积比盐湖城还要大，目前仍完全没有得到控制。

contentParagraphs.en: Firefighters could receive a small reprieve next week when cooler temperatures and higher humidities hit the region, according to the National Weather Service
contentParagraphs.zh: 据国家气象局称，下周该地区气温较低、湿度较高，消防队员可能会得到短暂的缓解

contentParagraphs.en: But the ongoing drought conditions and strain on resources due to multiple large fires compelled Utah Gov. Spencer Cox to issue an emergency order last week temporarily restricting firework displays through the Fourth of July holiday.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但持续的干旱和多起大火造成的资源紧张，迫使犹他州州长斯宾塞·考克斯上周发布紧急命令，在国庆节假期期间暂时限制烟花表演。

contentParagraphs.en: "When people who've dedicated their lives to protecting Utah tell us this year is different, we desperately need to listen," he said at a press conference announcing the restrictions.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他在宣布限制措施的新闻发布会上说：“当那些毕生致力于保护犹他州的人们告诉我们，今年的情况有所不同时，我们迫切需要倾听。”

contentParagraphs.en: The vast majority of wildfires in the U.S. are started by humans every year, according to the U.S. Forest Service. And the wildfires that do start are getting bigger and more destructive as a result of human activities that are warming the global climate.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据美国林务局称，美国每年绝大多数野火都是由人类引起的。由于人类活动导致全球气候变暖，确实发生的野火变得越来越大、更具破坏性。

contentParagraphs.en: A study, published earlier this week in the journal Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, found that forest fires now burn ten times more acreage annually than in 1985.
contentParagraphs.zh: 本周早些时候发表在《美国国家科学院院刊》杂志上的一项研究发现，现在每年森林火灾燃烧的面积是 1985 年的十倍。

contentParagraphs.en: Wildfires, in and of themselves, are not necessarily a bad thing. Fire is a natural phenomenon and many forests and ecosystems in the U.S. depend on low to moderate severity wildfires to clear out undergrowth and help propagate new trees.
contentParagraphs.zh: 野火本身并不一定是坏事。火灾是一种自然现象，美国的许多森林和生态系统依靠低至中度的野火来清除灌木丛并帮助繁殖新树木。

contentParagraphs.en: A century of aggressive wildfire suppression has caused many U.S. forests to become overgrown though. That legacy, combined with rising temperatures and shifting precipitation patterns — hallmarks of a warming world — have caused severe fires, resulting in widespread tree death, to now be more common than beneficial fires in California, the new study found.
contentParagraphs.zh: 然而，一个世纪以来积极扑灭野火已导致美国许多森林变得杂草丛生。新的研究发现，这一遗留问题，加上气温上升和降水模式变化（世界变暖的标志），导致了严重的火灾，导致大范围的树木死亡，现在在加利福尼亚州比有益的火灾更常见。

contentParagraphs.en: "The loss of these forests isn't just, 'I can't take a pretty picture,'" said Mitchell Hung, an earth-systems researcher who led the study as a graduate student at UCLA, in a statement. "There are profound socioeconomic impacts. Real dollars are being lost each year due to high severity forest fire."
contentParagraphs.zh: “这些森林的消失不仅仅是‘我无法拍出一张漂亮的照片’，”加州大学洛杉矶分校研究生领导这项研究的地球系统研究员米切尔·洪在一份声明中说。 “这会产生深远的社会经济影响。由于严重的森林火灾，每年都会造成实际的损失。”

articles.title: Will the new student loan limits actually drive down tuition? Economists weigh in
articles.summary: The idea that there's a connection between federal student loans and what colleges charge dates back almost four decades. But it's unclear that link can lead to lower costs.
articles.titleZh: 新的学生贷款限额真的会降低学费吗？经济学家也参与其中
articles.summaryZh: 联邦学生贷款和大学收费之间存在联系的想法可以追溯到近四十年前。但目前尚不清楚这种联系能否降低成本。

contentParagraphs.en: Students toss their mortarboards in the air at a 2018 commencement ceremony at Wesleyan University in Middletown, Conn.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在康涅狄格州米德尔敦卫斯理大学 2018 年毕业典礼上，学生们将学位帽抛向空中。

contentParagraphs.en: Eduardo Munoz Alvarez/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 爱德华多·穆尼奥斯·阿尔瓦雷斯/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: Eduardo Munoz Alvarez/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 爱德华多·穆尼奥斯·阿尔瓦雷斯/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: For the past two decades, graduate students have been able to take out an unlimited amount of federal student loans to cover the full cost of their education.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在过去的二十年里，研究生已经能够申请无限量的联邦学生贷款来支付他们的全部教育费用。

contentParagraphs.en: If they needed $60,000 a year, they could borrow $60,000 a year, year after year.
contentParagraphs.zh: 如果他们每年需要 60,000 美元，他们可以每年借 60,000 美元，年复一年。

contentParagraphs.en: The Trump administration has a plan to change that by capping graduate school loans for many students at $20,500 a year, and $100,000 overall — effective July 1. (A federal court temporarily blocked a small piece of that plan, but the U.S. Education Department confirmed to NPR that loan limits will indeed begin July 1.)
contentParagraphs.zh: 特朗普政府计划改变这一现状，将许多学生的研究生贷款上限限制为每年 20,500 美元，总体上限为 100,000 美元，并于 7 月 1 日生效。（联邦法院暂时阻止了该计划的一小部分，但美国教育部向 NPR 证实，贷款限制确实将于 7 月 1 日开始。）

contentParagraphs.en: In a year packed with changes to higher education policy, this new loan limit is one of the biggest, and one of the most controversial.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在高等教育政策不断发生变化的一年中，这一新的贷款限额是最大、也是最具争议的之一。

contentParagraphs.en: U.S. Secretary of Education Linda McMahon says the endgame is to force colleges to slash their tuition prices.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国教育部长琳达·麦克马洪表示，最终的结果是迫使大学削减学费。

contentParagraphs.en: "College costs are just exorbitant. Students are burdened with debt…" McMahon told the House education committee in May. "We really have to do something to bring down the cost of college."
contentParagraphs.zh: “大学费用太高了。学生背负着债务……”麦克马洪五月份告诉众议院教育委员会。 “我们确实必须采取一些措施来降低大学费用。”

contentParagraphs.en: With that goal in mind, Republicans used last year's One Big Beautiful Bill Act to scuttle the program known as Grad PLUS and limit graduate loans. The thinking goes: Borrowers will choose cheaper programs, and expensive schools will have to cut prices to compete.
contentParagraphs.zh: 考虑到这一目标，共和党人利用去年的一项美丽法案来破坏名为 Grad PLUS 的计划，并限制毕业生贷款。人们的想法是：借款人会选择更便宜的项目，而昂贵的学校将不得不降价来竞争。

contentParagraphs.en: But many economists aren't so sure it will do what Republicans say it will.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但许多经济学家不太确定它会像共和党所说的那样。

contentParagraphs.en: A decades-old idea
contentParagraphs.zh: 几十年前的想法

contentParagraphs.en: The idea that there's a connection between federal student loans and what colleges charge dates back almost four decades, to Feb. 18, 1987.
contentParagraphs.zh: 联邦学生贷款和大学收费之间存在联系的想法可以追溯到近四十年，即 1987 年 2 月 18 日。

contentParagraphs.en: That's the day then-Education Secretary William Bennett, under President Ronald Reagan, penned a scathing opinion piece for The New York Times, titled "Our Greedy Colleges."
contentParagraphs.zh: 就在这一天，罗纳德·里根总统领导下的时任教育部长威廉·贝内特为《纽约时报》撰写了一篇措辞严厉的评论文章，题为“我们贪婪的大学”。

contentParagraphs.en: In it, Bennett excoriated schools for tuition increases that outpaced inflation, and he argued that increases in federal student aid "have enabled colleges and universities blithely to raise their tuitions, confident that Federal loan subsidies would help cushion the increase."
contentParagraphs.zh: 贝内特在其中严厉批评学校的学费增长速度超过了通货膨胀，他认为联邦学生援助的增加“使学院和大学能够愉快地提高学费，相信联邦贷款补贴将有助于缓冲学费增长”。

contentParagraphs.en: His idea took hold, and economists have dubbed it "The Bennett Hypothesis."
contentParagraphs.zh: 他的想法得到了证实，经济学家将其称为“贝内特假说”。

contentParagraphs.en: "The Bennett Hypothesis essentially says that, if you provide greater federal aid to schools, they will respond by increasing the price," says Phillip Levine, a professor of economics at Wellesley College.
contentParagraphs.zh: “贝内特假说本质上是说，如果你向学校提供更多的联邦援助，他们会通过提高价格来回应，”韦尔斯利学院经济学教授菲利普·莱文说。

contentParagraphs.en: Almost 40 years later, Republicans are dusting off the Bennett Hypothesis to justify severe limits to student borrowing.
contentParagraphs.zh: 近 40 年后，共和党人重新提出贝内特假说，以证明严格限制学生借贷的合理性。

contentParagraphs.en: Graduate school is fueling the explosive growth of student debt
contentParagraphs.zh: 研究生院正在助长学生债务的爆炸性增长

contentParagraphs.en: To be clear, current limits on undergraduate loans aren't budging – and haven't budged in years. One reason: According to Levine, the net price for undergraduate programs – what families actually pay – has been pretty stagnant lately.
contentParagraphs.zh: 需要明确的是，目前对本科生贷款的限制并没有改变——而且多年来都没有改变。原因之一：根据莱文的说法，本科课程的净价格（家庭实际支付的费用）最近相当停滞。

contentParagraphs.en: "We've seen at the undergraduate level for at least the last five years or so that college costs have actually been fairly flat," says Preston Cooper, who studies higher education policy at the conservative-leaning American Enterprise Institute (AEI).
contentParagraphs.zh: 在保守倾向的美国企业研究所（AEI）研究高等教育政策的普雷斯顿·库珀（Preston Cooper）表示：“至少在过去五年左右的时间里，我们在本科阶段看到大学费用实际上相当稳定。”

contentParagraphs.en: But the cost of graduate school has increased considerably.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但研究生院的费用却大幅增加。

contentParagraphs.en: "We're at a point where almost half of the borrowing right now is among graduate students, despite them being a much smaller share of the overall population," says Robert Kelchen, a professor of higher education at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville.
contentParagraphs.zh: 田纳西大学诺克斯维尔分校高等教育学教授罗伯特·凯尔琴 (Robert Kelchen) 表示：“目前，几乎一半的借款都来自研究生，尽管他们在总人口中所占的比例要小得多。”

contentParagraphs.en: Which brings us to Grad PLUS, which the Trump administration plans to shut down on July 1.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这让我们想到了 Grad PLUS，特朗普政府计划于 7 月 1 日关闭该项目。

contentParagraphs.en: For two decades, Grad PLUS has worked as an add-on to the traditional loan program, allowing graduate students to effectively borrow as much as they needed – no limits or guardrails.
contentParagraphs.zh: 二十年来，Grad PLUS 一直作为传统贷款计划的附加项目，允许研究生有效地借贷所需金额——没有限制或护栏。

contentParagraphs.en: Cooper says it's not a stretch to think Grad PLUS helped fuel a rise in graduate school costs.
contentParagraphs.zh: 库珀表示，认为 Grad PLUS 助长了研究生院费用的上涨并不夸张。

contentParagraphs.en: "Up to this time, it has been a very easy answer [for schools] to basically increase revenues a little bit every year by just raising the cost of graduate school tuition because they know that the federal government is going to have to give their students a loan for those extra costs."
contentParagraphs.zh: “到目前为止，[对学校来说]一个非常简单的答案是，通过提高研究生院的学费，每年基本上增加一点收入，因为他们知道联邦政府将不得不向学生提供贷款来支付这些额外费用。”

contentParagraphs.en: What the research shows
contentParagraphs.zh: 研究表明

contentParagraphs.en: "Having essentially uncapped loans, I think, is not a great policy," says Jeff Denning, an economist and professor at the University of Texas at Austin.
contentParagraphs.zh: 得克萨斯大学奥斯汀分校经济学家兼教授杰夫·丹宁表示：“我认为，基本上无上限的贷款并不是一项伟大的政策。”

contentParagraphs.en: Denning was part of a team of researchers who studied the Grad PLUS program – to put the Bennett Hypothesis to the test. They wanted to know if, in Texas, the suddenly limitless font of Grad PLUS loans that began in 2006 contributed to graduate programs hiking their prices.
contentParagraphs.zh: 丹宁是研究 Grad PLUS 项目的研究小组的一员，该项目旨在检验贝内特假设。他们想知道，在德克萨斯州，2006 年开始的突然无限量的 Grad PLUS 贷款是否导致了研究生课程价格的上涨。

contentParagraphs.en: The short answer: Yes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 简短的回答：是的。

contentParagraphs.en: The researchers wrote that, for every additional dollar students received in loans, graduate schools increased their prices by $0.64 (after accounting for grants they gave out).
contentParagraphs.zh: 研究人员写道，学生每多获得一美元的贷款，研究生院的学费就会上涨 0.64 美元（考虑到他们发放的助学金后）。

contentParagraphs.en: Republicans often cite Denning's work as justification for ending Grad PLUS, arguing: If schools increased their prices nearly as much as federal aid increased, why wouldn't the opposite be true? Less aid should lead to lower prices.
contentParagraphs.zh: 共和党人经常引用丹宁的工作作为结束 Grad PLUS 的理由，他们认为：如果学校的价格上涨几乎与联邦援助的增加一样多，为什么相反的情况不会成立呢？援助减少应该会导致价格下降。

contentParagraphs.en: But it's not that simple, says the University of Tennessee's Kelchen, who has also researched the impact of Grad PLUS, specifically on business, medical and law schools.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但田纳西大学的 Kelchen 表示，事情并没有那么简单。他还研究了 Grad PLUS 的影响，特别是对商学院、医学院和法学院的影响。

contentParagraphs.en: "I did not find evidence" of a direct connection between federal aid and prices, Kelchen says.
contentParagraphs.zh: 凯尔琴说，“我没有发现”联邦援助与价格之间存在直接联系的证据。

contentParagraphs.en: Even Denning, when asked if the Bennett Hypothesis is true, says "it depends. I think there's some evidence that this happens in certain circumstances, and there's evidence that it doesn't."
contentParagraphs.zh: 甚至丹宁在被问及贝内特假设是否正确时也表示：“这要看情况。我认为有一些证据表明这种情况在某些情况下会发生，也有证据表明它不会。”

contentParagraphs.en: The Bennett Hypothesis is "a logical conclusion," according to Kelchen, "if you think that these graduate programs are massive profit centers." Some are, he says. Some aren't.
contentParagraphs.zh: 凯尔钦表示，“如果你认为这些研究生项目是巨大的利润中心”，贝内特假设就是“一个合乎逻辑的结论”。他说，有些是。有些则不然。

contentParagraphs.en: Medical school, for example, "is wildly unprofitable" for schools, Kelchen says. "It can take a million dollars of resources to produce one medical degree. So limiting borrowing is not going to reduce that cost."
contentParagraphs.zh: 例如，凯尔琴说，医学院对于学校来说“极其无利可图”。 “获得一个医学学位可能需要一百万美元的资源。因此，限制借贷并不会降低这一成本。”

contentParagraphs.en: Overall, he adds, evidence backing the Bennett Hypothesis "is largely mixed."
contentParagraphs.zh: 他补充说，总体而言，支持贝内特假说的证据“很大程度上是混杂的”。

contentParagraphs.en: Levine says much of the increase in the cost of higher
contentParagraphs.zh: 莱文表示，成本的增加大部分来自于更高的成本

articles.title: Social media bans go global: big tech faces a reckoning after Australia’s crackdown
articles.summary:
As a host of countries move to rein in social media use by children, could this be technology’s big tobacco moment?
 Continue reading...
articles.titleZh: 社交媒体禁令席卷全球：澳大利亚镇压后大型科技公司面临清算
articles.summaryZh:
随着许多国家开始限制儿童使用社交媒体，这可能是科技的大烟草时刻吗？
 继续阅读...

contentParagraphs.en: Quick GuideContributorsShowTiago Rogero in Rio de JaneiroSam Jones in Madrid Angelique Chrisafis in Paris Natasha May in BangkokHelena Smith in Athens Leyland Cecco in Toronto Hannah Ellis-Petersen in DelhiJennifer Rankin in Brussels Dan Milmo in LondonKate Connolly in Berlin Miranda Bryant Alastair McCready in TaipeiRachel Savage in Johannesburg Ankita Rao Nick Robins-EarlyWhen Australia launched its ban on social media for children under the age of 16 in December, its global impact remained to be seen. The world watched with keen interest as the country adopted the most far-reaching ban yet, amid a global mix of incredulity, admiration and – among some – a staunch belief that many children would find a way to circumvent it.Months later, it became clear that Australia’s efforts were the start of a global reckoning; in March, Indonesia began blocking children under the age of 16 from accessing most social media and Malaysia followed suit this month. Last week Britain announced its own ban, which it plans to have in place by early 2027.Australia’s ban has become a kind of “bellwether”, says Justin Hendrix, the chief executive and editor of Tech Policy Press, a nonprofit media venture that since February has been tracking efforts in more than 40 countries to ban children from accessing social media. “It certainly seemed to spark a curiosity among other regulators.”The risks of a laissez-faire approach have been laid bare by a mounting number of lawsuits. Photograph: Catherine Falls Commercial/Getty ImagesWhat exactly will come of all this remains to be seen, says Hendrix. “The phrase ‘tech’s “big tobacco” moment’ has been bandied about. It’s certainly the case that there is a more substantial body of evidence now than perhaps ever before about the harms and the addictive qualities of social media. But I would say that science is not entirely settled, we’re still learning.”From Greece to Gabon, each government effort to rein in social media use by children comes with its own nuances. In Austria, for example, the plan is to restrict access to those under 14 years, while France has set the age at 15. Norway, meanwhile, is looking to widen the age scope of its current ban from children under 13 to those 16 and under.In countries such as Poland, Denmark and England, the efforts to curb social media use have been paired with prohibitions on smartphones in schools. At times regulators have opted for one over the other, such as in Brazil, where authorities have banned the use of mobile phones in schools by pupils of all ages but allow children under 16 to have social media accounts as long as they are formally linked to a parent or legal guardian’s account.Map of social media restrictions globallygraphAll of it, however, points to a wider conversation that is being had about technology and its impact on children, says Hendrix. “There’s a huge amount of activity right now, contending with this extraordinary amount of technology that’s in children and teens’ lives, at home and at school. And when you step back, I think you see it as all of a piece: We don’t know what we’re doing.”In recent months the risks of a laissez-faire approach have been laid bare by the mounting lawsuits – lodged by school districts, government officials of all levels, and thousands of families around the world – accusing social media platforms of harming children’s mental health, whether through deliberate design choices that can be addicting or a failure to protect children from sexual predators and dangerous content. The companies behind the platforms have denied the allegations.A landmark case in California, in which Meta and YouTube were found liable for deliberately designing addictive products that hooked a young user and led to her being harmed, was particularly damning. “How do you make a child never put down the phone? That’s called the engineering of addiction. They engineered it, they put these features on the phones,” the lawyer Mark Lanier said during closing arguments.Amnesty International has described the Australia ban as an ‘ineffective quick fix’. Photograph: GoodLifeStudio/Getty ImagesAll of this has left a deep mark on society, says Hendrix. “I think the experience with social media, and to some extent the lag in the appearance of harms and the regulatory response, that’s weighing on people as they think of AI. You hear a lot of lawmakers saying: ‘We don’t want to repeat our mistakes.’”A handful of courts have sought to also tackle AI, from Britain whose recently announced ban includes plans to enforce a minimum age limit of 18 for romantic chatbots designed to simulate sexual relationships, to Canada where the crackdown on social media includes a requirement for the companies behind AI chatbots to put guardrails in place to lower the risk of them communicating harmful content. Recently Norway said it would impose a near-total ban on the use of generative AI tools in elementary schools and restrict their use among older students.The rush to regulate comes even as the overall impact of social media bans remains unclear. In Australia, while the government has said nearly 5m accounts identified as belonging to children have been shut down, a survey of 900 parents by the country’s online safety office found about two-thirds of young people who had social media accounts before the ban managed to retain access.Australia ban chartThe concerns were brushed off by Keir Starmer as he announced the UK government’s plans for a ban. “They get around other laws, too, but we don’t say: ‘Oh, look, a teenager managed to get a drink somehow, so let’s not bother banning alcohol sales to children,’” the prime minister said. “That would be utterly ridiculous, and so I just don’t accept that argument.”The rationale appeared to be less convincing for Italy’s prime minister, Giorgia Meloni, who said her government had no plans to introduce any kind of social media ban for children. “I am not against a social media ban for unde
contentParagraphs.zh: 快速指南贡献者节目蒂亚戈·罗杰罗在里约热内卢萨姆·琼斯在马德里安吉丽克·克里斯菲斯在巴黎娜塔莎·梅在曼谷海伦娜·史密斯在雅典利兰·切科在多伦多汉娜·埃利斯-彼得森在德里詹妮弗·兰金在布鲁塞尔丹·米尔莫在伦敦凯特·康诺利在柏林米兰达·布莱恩特·阿拉斯泰尔·麦克里迪在台北雷切尔·萨维奇在约翰内斯堡安基塔·拉奥尼克·罗宾斯-早期当澳大利亚推出社交媒体禁令时对于12月16岁以下儿童而言，其全球影响仍有待观察。全世界都以极大的兴趣注视着该国通过了迄今为止影响最深远的禁令，全球范围内充满了怀疑、钦佩，还有一些人坚信许多儿童会找到办法规避该禁令。几个月后，很明显，澳大利亚的努力是全球清算的开始； 3 月，印度尼西亚开始阻止 16 岁以下儿童访问大多数社交媒体，马来西亚本月也效仿。英国上周宣布了自己的禁令，计划在 2027 年初实施。Tech Policy Press 首席执行官兼编辑贾斯汀·亨德里克斯 (Justin Hendrix) 表示，澳大利亚的禁令已成为一种“风向标”。Tech Policy Press 是一家非营利性媒体企业，自 2 月份以来一直在跟踪 40 多个国家禁止儿童使用社交媒体的努力。 “这似乎确实引起了其他监管机构的好奇。” 越来越多的诉讼已经暴露出自由放任做法的风险。亨德里克斯说，这一切到底会带来什么仍有待观察。 “‘科技的‘大烟草’时刻’这个词已经被广泛流传。毫无疑问，现在有比以往任何时候都更多的证据来证明社交媒体的危害和成瘾性。但我想说的是，科学还没有完全解决，我们仍在学习。”从希腊到加蓬，政府为控制儿童使用社交媒体而做出的每一项努力都有自己的细微差别。例如，奥地利的计划是限制 14 岁以下的儿童使用，而法国则将年龄定为 15 岁。与此同时，挪威正在寻求将目前禁止 13 岁以下儿童的年龄范围扩大到 16 岁及以下。在波兰、丹麦和英国等国家，在限制社交媒体使用的同时，学校也禁止使用智能手机。有时监管机构会选择其中之一，例如在巴西，当局禁止所有年龄段的学生在学校使用手机，但允许 16 岁以下的儿童拥有社交媒体帐户，只要这些帐户与父母或法定监护人的帐户正式关联。亨德里克斯表示，全球社交媒体限制图谱显示，所有这些都表明人们正在就技术及其对儿童的影响进行更广泛的讨论。 “现在有大量的活动，与儿童和青少年生活中、家庭和学校中大量的技术作斗争。当你退后一步时，我想你会认为这是一个整体：我们不知道我们在做什么。”近几个月来，学区、各级政府官员和世界各地成千上万的家庭提出的越来越多的指控社交媒体的诉讼，暴露了自由放任做法的风险。损害儿童心理健康的平台，无论是通过可能令人上瘾的故意设计选择，还是未能保护儿童免受性掠夺者和危险内容的侵害。这些平台背后的公司否认了这些指控。加利福尼亚州发生了一起具有里程碑意义的案件，其中 Meta 和 YouTube 被判故意设计令人上瘾的产品，这些产品吸引了一名年轻用户并导致她受到伤害，这一案件尤其令人震惊。律师马克·拉尼尔（Mark Lanier）在结案陈词中表示：“如何让孩子永远不会放下手机？这就是所谓的成瘾工程。他们设计了它，将这些功能放在手机上。”国际特赦组织将澳大利亚禁令描述为“无效的快速解决方案”。亨德里克斯说，所有这些都给社会留下了深刻的印记。 “我认为，社交媒体的经历，以及某种程度上危害出现的滞后性和监管反应，让人们在考虑人工智能时感到压力。你听到很多立法者说：‘我们不想重复我们的错误。’”一些法院也试图解决人工智能问题，英国最近宣布的禁令包括计划对旨在模拟性关系的浪漫聊天机器人实施 18 岁的最低年龄限制，加拿大对社交媒体的打击包括对背后公司的要求人工智能聊天机器人可以设置护栏，以降低传播有害内容的风险。最近，挪威表示将几乎全面禁止在小学使用生成式人工智能工具，并限制年龄较大的学生使用这些工具。尽管社交媒体禁令的总体影响尚不清楚，但监管的热潮还是出现了。在澳大利亚，虽然政府表示已关闭近 500 万个被认定属于儿童的账户，但该国在线安全办公室对 900 名家长进行的一项调查发现，在禁令实施前拥有社交媒体账户的年轻人中，约有三分之二设法保留了访问权限。 澳大利亚禁令图表 Keir Starmer 在宣布英国政府的禁令计划时消除了这些担忧。 “他们也绕过了其他法律，但我们不会说：‘哦，看，一个青少年设法以某种方式喝到了一杯酒，所以我们不要费心禁止向儿童销售酒精饮料，’”总理说。 “这太荒谬了，所以我不接受这种说法。” 对于意大利总理乔治亚·梅洛尼来说，这个理由似乎不太令人信服，他表示，她的政府没有计划对儿童推出任何形式的社交媒体禁令。 “我并不反对针对未成年人的社交媒体禁令

articles.title: Three unusual things about the King's tax bill
articles.summary: King Charles paid £12.9m in tax for 2024-2025 - here's what we know about his unique tax situation.
articles.titleZh: 国王税单的三个不寻常之处
articles.summaryZh: 查尔斯国王在 2024 年至 2025 年期间缴纳了 1290 万英镑的税款 - 以下是我们对他独特的纳税情况的了解。

contentParagraphs.en: Image source, Getty ImagesByMitchell LabiakBusiness reporterKing Charles has made history by revealing his £12.9m tax bill, but the payment is far from ordinary.The announcement comes alongside the Royal Household publishing its annual financial report. Here's what the document tells us – and doesn't tell us – about the King's unique tax situation.1. The King pays some taxes voluntarilyKing Charles is not legally required to pay income tax, capital gains tax, or inheritance tax.Instead, he voluntarily pays some income tax, capital gains tax, and inheritance tax according to an agreement with the government called the Memorandum of Understanding (MoU)The MoU came about in 1993 following public pressure over the cost of running the Royal Family and is occasionally updated, most recently in 2023 to reflect the change of monarch following Queen Elizabeth II's death.The fact that some of the King's taxes are voluntary is not the case for regular taxpayers, and some argue this means that it is not a tax at all.HMRC defines tax as "money that individual people and businesses are legally required to pay to the government".Dan Neidle, founder of Tax Policy Associates, told the BBC: "If it's voluntary, it's not tax."Meanwhile, the report says King Charles pays VAT, employer taxes, and local rates "in line with requirements".2. We don't know how his tax bill was calculatedWhile the Royal Household describes releasing the King's tax bill as part of its "commitment to transparency", it's not clear how it has been figured out.So although we know that the King has agreed to pay tax on personal income, income from the Privy Purse not spent on official duties, and capital gains tax on private property sales, we don't know what proportion of those taxes make up the £12.9m paid.The Privy Purse is a source of private income for the ruling monarch.It mostly comprises income from the Duchy of Lancaster, an estate that belongs to whoever is the ruling monarch and includes, among other things, thousands of hectares of valuable land, castles and quarries.The report does say that the Privy Purse received £25.2m from the Duchy of Lancaster for the year to 31 March, but that is not all of the King's income. He also has personal earnings which the Royal Household says may include "investment income and trading profits". The report does not put a figure on this.Buckingham Palace described the move to publish the King's tax bill – as well as Prince William's – as increasing transparency which it said aimed to "encourage wider understanding of our accountability".Historian Anna Whitelock said the King revealing his tax bill puts him "front and centre as a very rich man"."I do think this is very much a sign of the times, and it's an attempt by the monarchy to try and get on the front foot and before they were absolutely pushed to try and show they are responsive and not reactive."However, Shaun Moore, tax and financial planning expert at wealth manager Quilter, said there's ultimately not much detail to look at in report."The headline figure is a large sum of tax and there's also a large sum of income quoted as well, but there's not any breakdown of about how that was arrived at."3. He can deduct official royal business from the billAnother thing not detailed in the report is what proportion of the Privy Purse income has been spent by the King personally and what proportion of it has been spent for official royal duties.This matters because the King only voluntarily pays tax on income spent personally, meaning the King can effectively deduct royal business from his tax bill.The King also does not pay tax on the Sovereign Grant, which is money paid from the Treasury to the Royal Household to pay for official duties.This system is a bit like how a self-employed person can file expenses on their self-assessment tax return for things like uniform or training.Except that the King has two tax-free ways in which he can fund official duties. Also, what counts as official duties is very different from what a regular self-employed taxpayer can expense.For example, the untaxed Sovereign Grant can be used to fund the staff costs and running expenses of the King's official household while untaxed official duties that can be paid by Privy Purse include the personal income of working members of the Royal Family.The Keeper of the Privy Purse, James Chalmers, said: "While Royal finances can sometimes appear complex, the underlying system is clear in principle, structured in law and refined over time to ensure the Monarch can serve with independence, accountability and in the long-term interests of the nation."Correction 27 June: A previous version of this story incorrectly said the Duchy of Lancaster estate owns the Savoy Hotel in London. This has been removed.
contentParagraphs.zh: 图片来源：Getty Images 作者：米切尔·拉比亚克 (Mitchell Labiak) 商业记者 查尔斯国王公布了 1290 万英镑的税单，创造了历史，但这笔款项远非平常。这一消息是在王室发布年度财务报告的同时发布的。以下是该文件告诉我们（以及没有告诉我们）的有关国王独特税收情况的内容。1。国王自愿缴纳一些税款法律上查尔斯国王并没有被要求缴纳所得税、资本利得税或遗产税。相反，他根据与政府达成的一项名为谅解备忘录 (MoU) 的协议，自愿缴纳一些所得税、资本利得税和遗产税。谅解备忘录于 1993 年在公众对王室运营成本的压力下制定，偶尔会更新，最近一次是在 2023 年，以反映伊丽莎白女王之后君主的更迭事实上，国王的一些税款是自愿的，但对于普通纳税人来说，情况并非如此，有些人认为这根本就不是税。英国税务海关总署将税收定义为“个人和企业依法必须向政府缴纳的钱”。税收政策协会创始人丹·奈德尔告诉英国广播公司：“如果是自愿的，那就不是税。”同时，报告称，查尔斯国王缴纳增值税、雇主税和地方税“符合要求”。2．我们不知道他的税单是如何计算的。虽然王室称发布国王的税单是其“透明度承诺”的一部分，但尚不清楚它是如何计算出来的。因此，尽管我们知道国王已同意对个人收入、未用于公务的私人钱包收入以及私人财产销售的资本利得税缴纳税款，但我们不知道这些税款在所支付的 1290 万英镑中占多少比例。私人钱包是执政君主的私人收入来源。它主要包括来自兰开斯特公国的收入，该庄园属于执政君主，其中包括数千公顷的宝贵土地、城堡和采石场。报告确实称，截至 3 月 31 日的一年里，枢密院从兰开斯特公国收到了 2520 万英镑，但这并不是国王的全部收入。他还有个人收入，王室称其中可能包括“投资收入和交易利润”。该报告没有具体说明这一点。白金汉宫将公布国王以及威廉王子的税收法案的举动描述为增加透明度，称其目的是“鼓励更广泛地了解我们的责任”。历史学家安娜·怀特洛克表示，国王披露他的税收法案使他“成为一个非常富有的人”。“我确实认为这在很大程度上是时代的标志，这是君主制试图在他们之前采取行动的尝试。 “然而，财富管理公司 Quilter 的税务和财务规划专家肖恩·摩尔 (Shaun Moore) 表示，报告中最终没有太多细节可供查看。“标题数字是一大笔税收，也引用了大量收入，但没有任何详细说明如何得出这一数字。”3。他可以从账单中扣除王室官方业务报告中没有详细说明的另一件事是，国王个人花费了私人钱包收入的多少比例，以及用于官方王室职责的比例。这很重要，因为国王只自愿对个人支出的收入纳税，这意味着国王可以有效地从他的税单中扣除王室业务。国王也不对主权拨款缴税，主权拨款是从财政部支付给王室用于支付公务的钱。这个系统有点像自营职业者如何在自我评估纳税申报表上提交制服或培训等费用。除了国王有两种免税方式可以为公务提供资金。此外，什么算作公务，与普通个体户纳税人可以支出的费用有很大不同。例如，免税的主权拨款可用于资助国王官方家庭的员工成本和日常开支，而私库可以支付的免税公务包括王室工作成员的个人收入。私人金库的守护者詹姆斯查尔默斯说：“虽然王室财务有时看起来很复杂，但底层系统原则上是明确的，法律结构并随着时间的推移不断完善，以确保君主能够独立、负责并符合国家的长期利益。” 6 月 27 日更正：这个故事的先前版本错误地称兰开斯特公爵领地拥有伦敦萨沃伊酒店。该内容已被删除。

articles.title: Europe's deadly heatwave breaks German record and halts public events
articles.summary: Germany's highest ever temperature of 41.3C is recorded provisionally in Saarbrücken, over the border from France.
articles.titleZh: 欧洲致命热浪打破德国记录并导致公共活动暂停
articles.summaryZh: 德国有史以来最高气温为 41.3 摄氏度，暂时记录在与法国接壤的萨尔布吕肯。

contentParagraphs.en: Temperatures broke June records in Germany, Belgium and the Netherlands on Friday as the death toll from Europe's heatwave climbed in Spain and France and authorities cancelled concerts and other public events because of health risks.Germany's highest ever temperature of 41.3C was recorded provisionally in the south-western city of Saarbrücken, just over the border from France, which this week endured its hottest temperatures three days in a row.Although France's heatwave has peaked, its health minister said the "emergence of deaths at home" was of particular concern.The World Meteorological Organization warned of "major impacts" to health, ecosystems, agriculture and labour.Spokeswoman Clare Nullis said: "We need to get used to it, unfortunately."Europe's deadly June heatwave has moved slowly north and east, and Belgian forecaster David Dehenauw said unofficially that 40C had been recorded in Kleine Brogel, close to the Dutch border.A top temperature of 39.4C was recorded in the southern Dutch province of Limburg. Meanwhile, in the UK a provisional June record of 37.1C was recorded at Cavendish in Suffolk.At least 150 million people across the European continent were facing temperatures higher than 35C on Friday, according to calculations by the AFP news agency.Czech meteorologists believe a 2012 record temperature of 40.4C could be broken on Saturday, while Austrian forecasters believe the national record will fall on Sunday. Balkan countries are seeing extreme heat too, with up to 39C forecast in Serbia over the weekend. In Switzerland, the Beznau nuclear power plant took both reactors off grid on Friday because the temperature in the River Aare reached 25C. The operator said the measure was to protect the environment, as water taken from the Aare to cool the reactors is returned to the river at a warmer temperature.Health fears led to the cancellation of two big Paris events at the weekend, following pressure from the local prefecture, which said the hospital system was "saturated" and resources had to be focused on "helping the most vulnerable".The organisers of Saturday's Paris Pride said they were looking to reschedule the march in September. The Solidays music festival at Longchamp racecourse had been due to start on Friday and run into Sunday, and attracted more than a quarter of a million visitors last year.However, a third event threatened with cancellation, the Diamond League athletics meeting at Charléty stadium, will go ahead on Sunday after organisers agreed to an "adapted format" involving moving the start later into the afternoon.Thousands of people had already arrived for Dutch music festival Defqon.1 on Thursday night when organisers said they had to pull the plug on the event because authorities had announced an unprecedented code red warning for extreme heat. Festival goers reacted angrily to the decision, prompting police to be called to respond to reports of unrest.Numerous events have also been cancelled in Germany, including Saturday's Hamburg Half Marathon.The French health minister has warned that the extreme conditions will result in additional deaths, and an emergency plan has been put in place for all hospitals in the Paris region because of "multiple tensions" in healthcare.In Marseille, an 18-month-old child died after being found in a car in a state of hyperthermia, in the latest such episode in France in a matter of days. A three-year-old became trapped in a car in Paris on Wednesday, and two young children lost their lives in a car in the southern town of Carpentras on Monday.The number of deaths by drowning in France since the heatwave began has risen to 55. An estimated two-thirds of them had been swimming in unsupervised areas.Spain's MoMo monitoring system, external for reporting temperature-related deaths has counted 327 fatalities between Sunday and Thursday that could be linked to the heat, and most of them have been recorded in the past two days.The high temperatures have begun to subside in Spain, however a forest fire broke out north-east of Barcelona forcing 16,000 to stay in their homes on Friday morning. A man was arrested on suspicion of arson.Little relief is expected in much of Europe over the weekend, and a glacier research team in Switzerland has warned that the heatwave is being felt in the mountains too.The Zurich team says almost all the winter reserves built up on the glaciers are about to run out, most likely on Monday, at which point the glaciers will start melting. That moment usually takes place in August, but Swiss researchers say the current course of glacial melting is almost as bad as in 2022, the worst year on record, when as much as 6% of the glaciers' mass was lost.Correction 27 June: This article has been updated to explain that the Beznau power plant was shut down on Friday, not because the temperature of water from the River Aare was too high to cool the reactors sufficiently, but because of the risk to the environment from water returned to the river after the cooling process.

articles.title: Food defined social hierarchy in 1776. Here's what was on the table
articles.summary: Around the time the United States was founded, Americans' diets included Parmesan ice cream and terrapin. But what you ate depended on your social status.
articles.titleZh: 1776 年，食物定义了社会等级制度。以下是餐桌上的食物
articles.summaryZh: 在美国建国前后，美国人的饮食包括帕尔马干酪冰淇淋和水龟。但你吃什么取决于你的社会地位。

contentParagraphs.en: Established in 1750, Middleton Tavern in Annapolis, Md., is one of the oldest continuously operating taverns in the U.S.
contentParagraphs.zh: 位于马里兰州安纳波利斯的 Middleton Tavern 成立于 1750 年，是美国持续经营的最古老的酒馆之一。

contentParagraphs.en: Anna Rose Layden for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的安娜·罗斯·莱登

contentParagraphs.en: Anna Rose Layden for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的安娜·罗斯·莱登

contentParagraphs.en: ANNAPOLIS, Md. — Arthur Gross has been the chef at Middleton Tavern in Annapolis, Md., for 50 years. That's a long time, even for a tavern that started serving food before the country's inception.
contentParagraphs.zh: 马里兰州安纳波利斯 — Arthur Gross 担任马里兰州安纳波利斯 Middleton Tavern 餐厅的厨师已经 50 年了。即使对于建国前就开始提供食物的小酒馆来说，这也是很长的时间了。

contentParagraphs.en: Sometimes, in between preparing food in the kitchen, "I wonder to myself what would it have been like?" Miller said of the early days at the 276-year-old tavern.
contentParagraphs.zh: 有时，在厨房准备食物的间隙，“我想知道那会是什么样子？”米勒谈到了这家拥有 276 年历史的小酒馆的早期时光。

contentParagraphs.en: In 1776, seafood such as rockfish and crab dominated the diets of many early colonists in Maryland.
contentParagraphs.zh: 1776 年，石斑鱼和螃蟹等海鲜在马里兰州许多早期殖民者的饮食中占据主导地位。

contentParagraphs.en: Arthur Gross is the executive chef at Middleton Tavern. He's worked at the restaurant for 50 years.
contentParagraphs.zh: 阿瑟·格罗斯 (Arthur Gross) 是米德尔顿酒馆 (Middleton Tavern) 的行政主厨。他在这家餐厅工作了 50 年。

contentParagraphs.en: Anna Rose Layden for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的安娜·罗斯·莱登

contentParagraphs.en: Anna Rose Layden for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的安娜·罗斯·莱登

contentParagraphs.en: And now, when Gross orders food for the restaurant, it features some of the same staples: 100 pounds of rockfish, 6 pounds of crab claw meat, 10 pounds of calamari rings and tentacles, crackers and lemon juice.
contentParagraphs.zh: 现在，当格罗斯为餐厅点餐时，提供的都是一些相同的主食：100 磅石斑鱼、6 磅蟹爪肉、10 磅鱿鱼圈和触手、饼干和柠檬汁。

contentParagraphs.en: In Annapolis, where it's common for historical reenactors decked out in short gowns, petticoats, caps and leather-buckle shoes to lead tourists around the historic streets, it almost feels like 1776. (If you squint a little and ignore the cars.)
contentParagraphs.zh: 在安纳波利斯，历史重演者经常穿着短袍、衬裙、帽子和皮扣鞋带领游客游览历史悠久的街道，感觉几乎就像 1776 年一样。（如果你眯着眼睛，忽略那些汽车的话。）

contentParagraphs.en: As America celebrates its 250th anniversary, it's hard not to imagine, along with Gross, how people's diets shaped everyday life then.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在美国庆祝建国 250 周年之际，我们很难不和格罗斯一起想象当时人们的饮食如何影响日常生活。

contentParagraphs.en: A reenactor dressed in Colonial-era garb enters Waterfront Warehouse with a group of schoolchildren in Annapolis.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一位身着殖民时代服装的重演者与一群小学生一起进入安纳波利斯的海滨仓库。

contentParagraphs.en: Anna Rose Layden for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的安娜·罗斯·莱登

contentParagraphs.en: Anna Rose Layden for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的安娜·罗斯·莱登

contentParagraphs.en: When we think about signs of wealth today, "we think about cars and handbags or real estate," said Dana Connett, the community programs coordinator at the nonprofit Historic Annapolis.
contentParagraphs.zh: 今天，当我们想到财富的标志时，“我们会想到汽车、手袋或房地产，”非营利组织历史悠久的安纳波利斯的社区项目协调员达纳·康内特 (Dana Connett) 说。

contentParagraphs.en: But in 1776, it was food. Two hundred and fifty years ago, social class defined the food that colonists and the enslaved ate.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但在 1776 年，它是食物。两百五十年前，社会阶级定义了殖民者和奴隶所吃的食物。

contentParagraphs.en: Despite the revolution, a desperation to remain like England
contentParagraphs.zh: 尽管发生了革命，但仍渴望保持英格兰的样子

contentParagraphs.en: Tables are set up with displays of upper-class dining at the William Paca House in Annapolis. Left: A table is set with fruit, nuts and cake. Right: Another table is set with dominoes and replica glasses of punch.
contentParagraphs.zh: 安纳波利斯的威廉·帕卡之家 (William Paca House) 的餐桌上摆满了上层阶级的餐饮展示。左：桌子上摆着水果、坚果和蛋糕。右：另一张桌子上摆着多米诺骨牌和潘趣酒复制品。

contentParagraphs.en: Anna Rose Layden for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的安娜·罗斯·莱登

contentParagraphs.en: Anna Rose Layden for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的安娜·罗斯·莱登

contentParagraphs.en: Down the street from Middleton Tavern, the William Paca House & Garden offers an intricate view of the life of one of Maryland's four signers of the Declaration of Independence. Paca, who served as Maryland's governor in the late 18th century, lived with his family in the home from about 1765 to 1780.
contentParagraphs.zh: 沿着米德尔顿酒馆 (Middleton Tavern) 的街道，威廉帕卡故居和花园 (William Paca House & Garden) 提供了马里兰州四位《独立宣言》签署人之一的复杂生活景观。帕卡曾于 18 世纪末担任马里兰州州长，大约在 1765 年至 1780 年间，他与家人在此居住。

contentParagraphs.en: The Founding Fathers and members of the gentry class relied heavily on recipes from France and England and even imported some of their food from Europe, food historians said. They were desperate to emulate the wealth and prestige of affluent Europeans.
contentParagraphs.zh: 食品历史学家表示，开国元勋和士绅阶级成员严重依赖法国和英国的食谱，甚至从欧洲进口一些食物。他们迫切希望效仿富裕的欧洲人的财富和声望。

contentParagraphs.en: At a dinner table in the Paca House, a representation of a spring meal sits on the table with original monogrammed silver condiment shakers. On this day, the Pacas would have eaten turkey soup, fried shad, collards, ham and asparagus, oysters and roast chicken.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在帕卡之家的餐桌上，桌上摆着春季大餐的摆设，上面还放着原创字母组合的银色调料瓶。这一天，帕卡斯人会吃火鸡汤、炸鲥鱼、羽衣甘蓝、火腿和芦笋、牡蛎和烤鸡。

contentParagraphs.en: The type of meat the colonists ate was a status symbol, and beef was the most sought-after, said Joyce White, a local food historian who set up the food displays at the Paca House. Chicken would be eaten on special occasions — to sacrifice a good hen rather than use it for its eggs would be a delicacy.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当地食品历史学家乔伊斯·怀特 (Joyce White) 在帕卡之家 (Paca House) 展示了食品，他说，殖民者吃的肉是身份的象征，而牛肉是最受欢迎的。鸡肉会在特殊的场合吃——牺牲一只好母鸡而不是用它来吃鸡蛋会是一种美味。

contentParagraphs.en: "If it's your pig or cow coming from your plantation, you don't want to waste it," White said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “如果是来自你的种植园的猪或牛，你不想浪费它，”怀特说。

contentParagraphs.en: A replica of a kitchen used by enslaved people to prepare food, including rabbit, oysters and bread, is seen at the Paca House.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在帕卡之家可以看到一个厨房的复制品，奴隶们用来准备食物，包括兔子、牡蛎和面包。

contentParagraphs.en: Anna Rose Layden for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的安娜·罗斯·莱登

contentParagraphs.en: Anna Rose Layden for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的安娜·罗斯·莱登

contentParagraphs.en: Food was served on small plates, not the huge portions we normally see today, according to Adrian Miller. He's the author of The President's Kitchen Cabinet, which tells the stories of the African Americans who served presidents.
contentParagraphs.zh: 阿德里安·米勒 (Adrian Miller) 表示，食物是装在小盘子里的，而不是我们今天通常看到的大份食物。他是《总统的厨房橱柜》一书的作者，该书讲述了为总统服务的非裔美国人的故事。

contentParagraphs.en: The diets of the early colonists differed based on regional availability, but seafood dominated many plates as well. "There was just like this treasure trove of seafood that was fresh, and it wasn't polluted," Miller said. So many oysters were eaten that they were included in the construction of houses and roads, he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 早期殖民者的饮食因地区供应情况而异，但海鲜也占据了许多菜肴的主导地位。米勒说：“这里的海鲜宝库很新鲜，而且没有受到污染。”他说，人们吃掉了太多的牡蛎，以至于它们被用于建造房屋和道路。

contentParagraphs.en: On the coast of Maryland, terrapin would have been a popular food plucked right from the Chesapeake Bay, he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他说，在马里兰州海岸，从切萨皮克湾捕获的水龟是一种很受欢迎的食物。

contentParagraphs.en: The Founding Fathers rejected a monarchy but dined like kings
contentParagraphs.zh: 开国元勋们拒绝君主制，但却像国王一样用餐

contentParagraphs.en: Founding Fathers, including Thomas Jefferson and George Washington, helped shape food culture 250 years ago, according to food historians.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据食品历史学家称，托马斯·杰斐逊和乔治·华盛顿等国父们在 250 年前就帮助塑造了饮食文化。

contentParagraphs.en: Library of Congress
contentParagraphs.zh: 国会图书馆

contentParagraphs.en: Library of Congress
contentParagraphs.zh: 国会图书馆

contentParagraphs.en: Thomas Jefferson, George Washington and a few others defined the food of the time period, food historians said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 食品历史学家表示，托马斯·杰斐逊、乔治·华盛顿和其他一些人定义了那个时期的食品。

contentParagraphs.en: "The Founding Fathers were considered very much trendsetters, and a lot of them are Francophiles," said Sarah Lohman, a food historian and author of Eight Flavors: The Untold Story of American Cuisine.
contentParagraphs.zh: “国父们被认为是潮流引领者，其中很多人都是法国人，”食品历史学家、《八种口味：美国烹饪不为人知的故事》一书的作者莎拉·洛曼 (Sarah Lohman) 说。

contentParagraphs.en: From Jefferson, recipes of macaroni and cheese and beef à la mode still exist, Lohman said. John Adams, on the other hand, was a "very simple man," and he "got super irritated" with Jefferson because of his French wine and decadent food.
contentParagraphs.zh: 洛曼说，来自杰斐逊的通心粉、奶酪和现代牛肉的食谱仍然存在。另一方面，约翰·亚当斯是一个“非常简单的人”，他对杰斐逊“非常恼火”，因为他的法国葡萄酒和颓废的食物。

contentParagraphs.en: "Jefferson loved macaroni," said Ashley Rose Young, another food historian, who is an American history curator at the Library of Congress. Historical records show Jefferson toying around with the schematics for a macaroni-maker, she said. Jefferson was also eating black-eyed peas, turnip greens and ham.
contentParagraphs.zh: “杰斐逊喜欢通心粉，”另一位食品历史学家、美国国会图书馆的美国历史馆长阿什利·罗斯·杨 (Ashley Rose Young) 说。她说，历史记录显示杰斐逊正在摆弄通心粉制造商的示意图。杰斐逊还吃黑眼豌豆、萝卜青菜和火腿。

contentParagraphs.en: Thomas Jefferson's drawing of a macaroni machine and instructions for making pasta, circa 1787.
contentParagraphs.zh: 托马斯·杰斐逊 (Thomas Jefferson) 绘制的通心粉机和意大利面制作说明，约 1787 年。

contentParagraphs.en: Thomas Jefferson Papers/Library of Congress
contentParagraphs.zh: 托马斯·杰斐逊论文/国会图书馆

contentParagraphs.en: Thomas Jefferson Papers/Library of Congress
contentParagraphs.zh: 托马斯·杰斐逊论文/国会图书馆
