articles.title: GTA 6 will launch as download only - does that mean the disc is dead?
articles.summary: Music and films are now largely digital-only - does the lack of a disc for GTA 6 mean gaming is going the same way?
articles.titleZh: GTA 6 将仅以下载方式启动 - 这是否意味着光盘已失效？
articles.summaryZh: 音乐和电影现在基本上都是数字化的——《GTA 6》缺少光盘是否意味着游戏也会走同样的路？

contentParagraphs.en: GTA 6 will launch as download only - does that mean the disc is dead?Image source, Rockstar GamesImage caption, Rockstar revealed that the price of GTA 6 will be £70 for a standard edition and £90 for its more premium editionByLaura CressTechnology reporterOnce, video games came with lots of physical goodies, such as guides, maps and manuals.Those days are mostly gone, but gamers have, up to now, usually been able to rely on one thing they could literally get their hands on - a disc.But when pre-orders for Grand Theft Auto 6 went live on Thursday, developer Rockstar said customers who opted for the physical edition would get a box that just contained a code for a digital download instead.It's not the first time a physical edition has launched this way.But if the makers of one of the biggest franchises in entertainment history has decided to follow suit, does that mean the disc is dead? And what does that say about game ownership in the digital age?Unacceptable - or inevitable?"My initial reaction was one of confusion and shock," Ben, a UK-based 24-year-old gamer who covers GTA news on social media under the handle 'videotech', told me following the announcement.In an interview with Variety, external in February, Strauss Zelnick, the chief executive of Take-Two, Rockstar's parent company, had said a digital-only launch was "not the plan".Ben said this led many to hope the physical disc would be available at launch too - and for more than sentimental reasons."An important benefit of owning a disc is that you can lend the game to a friend or sell it later," he said.Online retailers selling the physical edition have stated that, as with other digital game codes, the one being offered for GTA 6 is single-use and will become invalid after it is redeemed.Image source, Rockstar GamesImage caption, GTA 6 is set in a fictional US state called Leonida, which is Rockstar's version of FloridaDigital sales now account for the vast majority of game revenue, and online stores such as Steam for PC and the PlayStation Store have been around for many years. The addition of consoles without physical disc drives is only helping further this trend, said Mat Piscatella, senior director and video game industry advisor at market research firm Circana."More than half of all Xbox Series consoles in the US don't have a physical drive, while over a quarter of PS5's are the same," he posted, external.For some gamers, the news they wouldn't be able to play GTA 6 on a disc therefore felt like less of an upset."I'm pretty much all digital these days, I download most of my games directly onto my console," Alogirlx, a gaming content creator from Canada, told the BBC's What in the World podcast."Whether there's a physical disc or not it doesn't really change anything for me," she said.With physical game sales at an all-time low, the issue may well be less about the disc itself than what its absence may represent.For some, it appears to be another step away from buying a physical product, and instead towards buying a licence or access to software controlled by the publisher and platform holder.Ross Scott is the founder of the growing consumer rights movement Stop Killing Games.The group is campaigning for publishers to leave online games playable if they end up switching off their servers.He told me the issue raised by Rockstar's code-in-a-box is less about the missing disc itself - after all, an online game on a disc can still become unplayable if its servers are shut down.Instead, he said, it was about a lack of trust that publishers will preserve access to games consumers have already paid for."The problem is the industry has a very poor reputation of disabling games once they end support, so the trust from customers for many large publishers just isn't there," he said."I don't think the lack of a disc is the problem in itself, but rather it can be a symptom of a larger, very consumer-hostile practice."Why might Rockstar have done this?The BBC has reached out to Rockstar for more details on its decision, but it is yet to comment.Chris Scullion, deputy editor of Video Games Chronicle, pointed to previous data leaks suffered by the studio as a possible explanation for why it may want to ensure its content cannot be easily ripped from a disc and shared before release.He added that the game, which has already been delayed twice, may also be "so close to the wire" in development that players could still get an out-of-date version if they picked up a disc rather than the digital edition."A cynic would also say it's simply a way to make more money on each physical copy sold," he said.Rockstar is not the only company moving away from the traditional physical format, however.Nintendo has also shifted further towards digital distribution in recent years with the introduction of Game-Key Cards - physical cartridges that act as a key to download a game, rather than containing the game itself.Image source, AlogirlxImage caption, With physical game sales at an all-time low, many gamers like Alogirlx are used to downloading games rather than buying the physical versionWhat have physical retailers said?The GTA franchise is one of the biggest and most profitable entertainment properties in history.GTA V has sold nearly 230 million copies, and the launch of its successor is expected to be such a major economic event that Bank of America has weighed in on its price, external.Despite this, Rockstar's announcement has prompted some independent retailers to refuse to stock the code-in-a-box version.VGP, an online retailer with a physical store in Toronto, said, external while it had "tremendous respect" for Rockstar, it was "committed to preserving the value of physical game ownership", so would not be offering the product.Lootbox Gaming, an independent retailer in Delaware, also declined to stock it, telling me the decision "speaks volumes about the future for AAA (big-budget) releases on physical media". Meanwhile, PNP Games, an online reta
contentParagraphs.zh: GTA 6 将仅以下载方式推出 - 这是否意味着该光盘已失效？ 图片来源，Rockstar Games 图片说明，Rockstar 透露，GTA 6 标准版价格为 70 英镑，高级版价格为 90 英镑 作者：Laura Cress 科技记者 曾经，视频游戏附带大量实物物品，例如指南、地图和手册。那些日子已经一去不复返了，但到目前为止，游戏玩家通常能够依赖他们可以真正获得的东西但是，当《侠盗猎车手 6》周四开始预购时，开发商 Rockstar 表示，选择实体版的客户将收到一个只包含数字下载代码的盒子。这并不是实体版第一次以这种方式推出。但是，如果娱乐史上最大的特许经营权之一的制造商决定效仿，这是否意味着这张光盘已经死了？这对数字时代的游戏所有权意味着什么？这是不可接受的，还是不可避免的？“我的第一反应是困惑和震惊，”Ben 是一位居住在英国的 24 岁游戏玩家，他在社交媒体上以“videotech”为账号报道 GTA 新闻。在 2 月份接受外部 Variety 采访时，Rockstar 母公司 Take-Two 的首席执行官 Strauss Zelnick 表示，纯数字发行“不是Ben 表示，这让许多人希望实体光盘也能在发售时推出，这不仅仅是出于情感原因。“拥有光盘的一个重要好处是，你可以将游戏借给朋友或稍后出售。”他说。销售实体版的在线零售商表示，与其他数字游戏代码一样，GTA 6 提供的代码是一次性的，在兑换后将失效。图片来源，Rockstar Games 图片说明，GTA 6 的背景设定在一个虚构的美国名为 Leonida 的州，即 Rockstar 版本的 FloridaDigital 销售现在占游戏收入的绝大多数，PC 版 Steam 和 PlayStation Store 等在线商店也已存在多年。市场研究公司 Circana 的高级总监兼视频游戏行业顾问马特·皮斯卡特拉 (Mat Piscatella) 表示，增加没有物理光驱的游戏机只会进一步推动这一趋势。“美国超过一半的 Xbox 系列游戏机没有物理光驱，而超过四分之一的 PS5 都是相同的。”他在外部发帖称。对于一些游戏玩家来说，他们无法在光盘上玩 GTA 6 的消息并没有那么令人沮丧。来自加拿大的游戏内容创作者 Alogirlx 告诉 BBC 的 What in the World 播客。“几天来，我将大部分游戏直接下载到我的游戏机上。”她说，“无论是否有实体光盘，对我来说都没有真正改变任何事情。”由于实体游戏销量处于历史最低水平，问题很可能不是光盘本身，而是光盘的缺失所代表的意义。对于某些人来说，这似乎是远离购买实体产品的又一步，而是购买许可证或访问权罗斯·斯科特 (Ross Scott) 是日益壮大的消费者权益运动“停止杀戮游戏”(Stop Killing Games) 的创始人。该组织正在呼吁发行商在关闭服务器后仍可玩在线游戏。他告诉我，Rockstar 盒内代码提出的问题与丢失光盘本身无关——毕竟，如果服务器被关闭，光盘上的在线游戏仍然可能无法玩。相反，他说，这是因为发行商对保留对游戏的访问权缺乏信任。他说：“问题在于，该行业在游戏一旦结束支持后就将其禁用的名声很差，因此客户对许多大型发行商的信任不复存在。”“我不认为缺少光盘本身就是问题，而是它可能是一种更大规模、对消费者非常敌视的做法的症状。”为什么 Rockstar 会这样做？英国广播公司 (BBC) 已联系 Rockstar 了解有关其决定的更多细节，但尚未发表评论。 《Video Games Chronicle》副主编 Scullion 指出，该工作室此前遭受的数据泄露事件可能是其希望确保其内容在发布前不会被轻易从光盘上盗取并共享的一个可能解释。他补充说，这款已经被推迟两次的游戏在开发过程中也可能“接近开发进度”，如果玩家选择光盘而不是数字版本，他们仍然可能会得到过时的版本。然而，Rockstar 并不是唯一一家放弃传统实体格式的公司。任天堂近年来还进一步转向数字发行，推出了游戏密钥卡 - 物理卡带充当下载游戏的密钥，而不是包含游戏本身。图片来源，Alogirlx 图片说明，由于实体游戏销量处于历史最低水平，像 Alogirlx 这样的许多游戏玩家习惯于下载游戏而不是购买实体版本 实体零售商怎么说？ 《GTA》系列是历史上规模最大、利润最高的娱乐作品之一。《GTA V》已售出近 2.3 亿份，其继任者的推出预计将成为一场重大经济事件，以至于美国银行在外部对其价格进行了权衡。尽管如此，Rockstar 的声明还是促使一些独立零售商拒绝销售盒装代码版本。在多伦多设有实体店的在线零售商 VGP 对外表示，虽然它对《GTA V》抱有“极大的尊重”。 Rockstar 表示，它“致力于保护实体游戏所有权的价值”，因此不会提供该产品。特拉华州的一家独立零售商 Lootbox Gaming 也拒绝进货，并告诉我，这一决定“充分说明了 AAA（大预算）在实体媒体上发行的未来”。与此同时，PNP Games，一个在线娱乐场

articles.title: Australian Prof. Ajay Narendra discusses his team's discovery of the 'ballista spider'
articles.summary: Prof. Ajay Narendra from Macquarie University in Australia tells NPR about his team's discovery of a particularly rare, and high-powered, hunting method of the informally named "ballista spider."
articles.titleZh: 澳大利亚教授阿贾伊·纳伦德拉 (Ajay Narendra) 讨论他的团队发现的“弩炮蜘蛛”
articles.summaryZh: 澳大利亚麦格理大学的阿贾伊·纳伦德拉 (Ajay Narendra) 教授向 NPR 讲述了他的团队发现了一种特别罕见且威力强大的狩猎方法，这种方法被非正式地命名为“弩炮蜘蛛”。

contentParagraphs.en: Prof. Ajay Narendra from Macquarie University in Australia tells NPR about his team's discovery of a particularly rare, and high-powered, hunting method of the informally named "ballista spider."
contentParagraphs.zh: 澳大利亚麦格理大学的阿贾伊·纳伦德拉 (Ajay Narendra) 教授向 NPR 讲述了他的团队发现了一种特别罕见且威力强大的狩猎方法，这种方法被非正式地命名为“弩炮蜘蛛”。

contentParagraphs.en: (SOUNDBITE OF TV SHOW, "SUPERMAN (1940S ANIMATED FILM SERIES")UNIDENTIFIED ACTOR #1: (As character) In the sky. It's a bird.UNIDENTIFIED ACTOR #2: (As character) It's a plane.UNIDENTIFIED ACTOR #3: (As character) It's Superman.SCOTT SIMON, HOST: Well, no, it's not wearing a cape and, kablooey, it gets trapped in a spider's web. You see, this isn't a "Superman" movie - more like a horror film with a tarantula.(SOUNDBITE OF TV SHOW, "SUPERMAN (1940S ANIMATED FILM SERIES")UNIDENTIFIED ACTOR #4: (As character, screams).SIMON: And it's real life, and how a newly discovered species of spider captures green ants in Australia.AJAY NARENDRA: My name's professor Ajay Narendra. I'm a professor of insect neuroethology. I'm at the School of Natural Sciences Macquarie University in Sydney, Australia.SIMON: Professor Narendra and his colleagues published a paper this week that details the ballistic speed with which a new spider hunts its prey with a hundred percent observed success rate.NARENDRA: Most spiders typically avoid hunting ants because ants are considered to be very dangerous animals for spiders because ants have several numbers in a single colony.SIMON: But those still unnamed spiders do not shy away from a challenge. Not only do they hunt some of the most aggressive ants Australia has to offer. They do so with engineering finesse.NARENDRA: It takes about three to four hours for it to build an entire web high up from the ground. And then, as it builds the web, it starts to use tension lines, so all made of silk. And it starts to build a cone-like structure on a substrate. It could be a leaf in close proximity to these aggressive green tree ants.SIMON: So the spider builds a cone-shaped receptacle, and using more webbing, it ties down that cone to a leaf very tightly, like a spring-loaded trap. And then it adds an outer layer of silk that mysteriously attracts only green tree ants. And the ants? Well...NARENDRA: They dislodge this conical snare, and because the spider has made the silk quite sticky, the ant cannot let go. And because the spider has also added so much of tension on these silk lines, it literally propels the ant up from the substrate back to its core web - absolutely unheard of and brilliant.SIMON: Well, brilliant for us. The ant is catapulted. Using fast cameras, professor Narendra and his colleagues calculated that the ants fly at amazing speeds.NARENDRA: The acceleration at which the ant gets propelled is about 1,360 meters per second squared. That's equal to about 140 G's.SIMON: A hundred forty times the force of gravity. That's over five times what trained fighter pilots can handle. Ha - try that, Tom Cruise.(SOUNDBITE OF FIGHTER JET ACCELERATING)SIMON: Remarkably, the ant survives impact, but it remains trapped until the spider decides to eat it. The spiders are part of the Propostira genus of spiders. They're new to Australia, and professor Narendra says these mavericks have never been seen hunting like this before. For now, the professor says they've given the spider an informal name after a similar ancient weapon.NARENDRA: The best analogy we could think of was the medieval Roman weapon, which is the ballist. And we said, let's call it the ballista spider.SIMON: Narendra says the study of ballista spider is still in its early days, but there could be some practical applications based on what they discover from how these spiders trap their prey and how the ants survive a g-force that is double that of surface-to-air missiles. Nature, as they say, is metal.(SOUNDBITE OF SONG, "DANGER ZONE GOES HEAVY")NO RESOLVE AND STATE OF MINE: (Singing) Oh.
contentParagraphs.zh: （电视节目原声片段，“超人（1940 年代动画电影系列”）身份不明的演员 #1：（作为角色）在天空中。这是一只鸟。身份不明的演员 #2：（作为角色）这是一架飞机。身份不明的演员 #3：（作为角色）这是超人。斯科特·西蒙，主持人：嗯，不，它没有穿着你看，这不是一部“超人”电影 - 更像是一部有狼蛛的恐怖电影。（电视节目原声，“超人（1940 年代动画电影系列”）身份不明的演员 #4：（作为角色，尖叫）。 西蒙：这是现实生活，以及一种新发现的蜘蛛如何捕获绿色蚂蚁。 AJAY NARENDRA：我是澳大利亚悉尼麦考瑞大学自然科学学院的昆虫神经行为学教授。SIMON：Narendra 教授和他的同事本周发表了一篇论文，详细介绍了新蜘蛛捕猎猎物的弹道速度，观察到的成功率高达 100%。NARENDRA：大多数蜘蛛通常会避免捕猎蚂蚁，因为蚂蚁被认为对蜘蛛来说是非常危险的动物。因为蚂蚁在一个群体中拥有多个数量。 西蒙：但那些尚未命名的蜘蛛不仅会捕猎澳大利亚提供的一些最具攻击性的蚂蚁，而且还会通过巧妙的工程技术来实现这一目标。 纳伦德拉：它需要大约三到四个小时才能从地面上构建出整个网。它可能是靠近这些攻击性绿树蚂蚁的叶子。西蒙：所以蜘蛛会建造一个圆锥形的容器，并使用更多的织带，将圆锥形紧紧地绑在叶子上，就像一个弹簧加载的陷阱，然后它会神秘地吸引只绿树蚂蚁的外层丝。因为蜘蛛在这些丝线上施加了很大的张力，它实际上将蚂蚁从基质上推回其核心网 - 绝对是闻所未闻的，这真是太棒了。 西蒙：嗯，对我们来说太棒了。纳伦德拉教授和他的同事使用快速摄像机计算出蚂蚁以惊人的速度飞行。纳伦德拉：蚂蚁被推进的加速度约为每秒 1,360 米。这相当于大约 140 G。 西蒙：这是训练有素的战斗机飞行员可以承受的力的五倍多，汤姆·克鲁斯，试试吧。（战斗机加速的声音片段）西蒙：值得注意的是，蚂蚁在撞击中幸存下来，但它仍然被困住，直到蜘蛛决定吃掉它。纳伦德拉教授说，这些特立独行的人以前从未见过这样的狩猎方式，教授说他们已经给这种蜘蛛起了一个非正式的名字，以类似的古代武器命名。纳伦德拉：我们能想到的最好的类比是中世纪的罗马武器，那就是弩炮蜘蛛。西蒙：纳伦德拉说，对弩炮蜘蛛的研究仍处于早期阶段，但根据他们从这些蜘蛛如何捕获它们的方式中发现的结果，可能会有一些实际应用。猎物以及蚂蚁如何在两倍于地对空导弹的重力下生存，正如他们所说，大自然是金属。（歌曲原声片段，“危险地带沉重”）没有决心和我的状态：（唱歌）哦。

articles.title: "If you are alive, make any noise": Venezuela searches rubble on day four
articles.summary: In Venezuela, rescue teams are racing against time after twin earthquakes killed more than 1,400 people and left tens of thousands unaccounted for.
articles.titleZh: “如果你还活着，就发出任何声音”：委内瑞拉第四天在废墟中搜寻
articles.summaryZh: 在委内瑞拉，两次地震造成 1,400 多人死亡、数万人下落不明，救援队正在与时间赛跑。

contentParagraphs.en: Venezuelan firefighters and volunteers search for possible victims atop a collapsed building in Caraballeda, La Guaira state, Venezuela, on Saturday, June 27, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 6 月 27 日星期六，委内瑞拉消防员和志愿者在委内瑞拉拉瓜伊拉州卡拉巴莱达一座倒塌的建筑物顶上寻找可能的遇难者。

contentParagraphs.en: CARACAS, Venezuela — You can hear backhoes digging through concrete and twisted metal, as rescue workers call out into the wreckage.
contentParagraphs.zh: 委内瑞拉加拉加斯——你可以听到反铲挖土机挖掘混凝土和扭曲金属的声音，救援人员在残骸中呼喊。

contentParagraphs.en: "We are the rescue team. If you are alive please make any noise."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们是救援队。如果你还活着，请发出声音。”

contentParagraphs.en: A middle-aged man in a blue shirt looks on in desperation. He still has hope someone he knows on the sixth floor will be alive.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一名身穿蓝色衬衫的中年男子绝望地看着。他仍然希望六楼的一个认识的人还活着。

contentParagraphs.en: Across the street from another shattered building, Junior Laya and Jesus Gallardo sit on the shade, covered in dust.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在另一座破碎建筑的街对面，朱尼尔·拉亚和赫苏斯·加拉多坐在阴凉处，浑身都是灰尘。

contentParagraphs.en: "I have family members missing… my brother and some cousins," Gallardo says. "We don't know anything about them. We've been looking for them for four days."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我的家人失踪了……我的兄弟和一些堂兄弟姐妹，”加拉多说。 “我们对他们一无所知。我们已经找他们四天了。”

contentParagraphs.en: It is now four days since powerful twin earthquakes left parts of Venezuela in ruins.
contentParagraphs.zh: 四天前，委内瑞拉部分地区发生强烈地震，部分地区变成废墟。

contentParagraphs.en: Damaged buildings are seen three days after earthquakes struck in La Guaira, Venezuela, Saturday, June 27, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 6 月 27 日星期六，委内瑞拉拉瓜伊拉发生地震三天后，建筑物受损。

contentParagraphs.en: On Saturday, the government said the death toll had reached 1,430, with nearly 3,500 injured. Those figures are expected to rise. Authorities say thousands remain missing, as the critical window to find survivors continues to narrow.
contentParagraphs.zh: 周六，政府表示死亡人数已达到 1,430 人，近 3,500 人受伤。这些数字预计还会上升。当局表示，随着寻找幸存者的关键窗口继续缩小，仍有数千人失踪。

contentParagraphs.en: Cargo planes carrying emergency aid for earthquake victims are now beginning to land in Venezuela, after the main airport in the capital Caracas partially reopened to relief flights.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在首都加拉加斯的主要机场部分重新开放救援航班后，为地震灾民运送紧急援助物资的货机现已开始降落在委内瑞拉。

contentParagraphs.en: On the ground in La Guaira, a coastal state just north of Caracas, one of the areas closest to the earthquake's epicenter, it's utter devastation.
contentParagraphs.zh: 加拉加斯以北的沿海州拉瓜伊拉是距离地震震中最近的地区之一，这里的地面已被彻底摧毁。

contentParagraphs.en: Buildings are pancaked, their floors stacked like decks of cards.
contentParagraphs.zh: 建筑物像煎饼一样，地板像一副扑克牌一样堆放。

contentParagraphs.en: Dozens of people are digging through the rubble with whatever they can find—mostly small shovels and their bare hands. They are told someone is still inside, though it is not clear whether that person is alive or dead, or even who they are. Only their feet are visible.
contentParagraphs.zh: 数十人正在用他们能找到的任何东西在废墟中挖掘——大部分是小铲子和他们的徒手。他们被告知有人仍在里面，但不清楚那个人是活着还是死了，甚至不清楚他们是谁。只有他们的脚是可见的。

contentParagraphs.en: Helicopters buzz in the sky.
contentParagraphs.zh: 直升机在天空中嗡嗡作响。

contentParagraphs.en: People wait next to damaged structures, hoping to hear news of anyone who might get pulled from the rubble.
contentParagraphs.zh: 人们在受损的建筑旁等待，希望听到有人从废墟中被救出的消息。

contentParagraphs.en: Andrea Peña is a 34-year-old resident of La Guaira, whose home was destroyed.
contentParagraphs.zh: 安德里亚·佩纳 (Andrea Peña) 是拉瓜伊拉 (La Guaira) 的 34 岁居民，他的房屋被毁。

contentParagraphs.en: She's under a tent, outside the debris of her 7-floor building, guarding shovels and hand saws to give to rescue workers when they shout for them.
contentParagraphs.zh: 她在七层建筑废墟外的一个帐篷下，守卫着铲子和手锯，以便在救援人员呼喊时向他们提供。

contentParagraphs.en: "My dad and my nephew are trapped there, she says. The day the earthquake hit was a holiday in Venezuela, so she was out in the street celebrating. That's how she survived.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我的父亲和我的侄子被困在那里，”她说。地震发生的那天是委内瑞拉的节日，所以她在街上庆祝。这就是她幸存下来的原因。

contentParagraphs.en: A youth rescued from the debris is taken away on a motorcycle two days after earthquakes struck La Guaira, Venezuela, Friday, June 26, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 6 月 26 日星期五，委内瑞拉拉瓜伊拉发生地震两天后，一名从废墟中获救的年轻人被一辆摩托车带走。

contentParagraphs.en: "My nephew was sleeping and my dad was watching the World Cup," she says.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我的侄子正在睡觉，而我的父亲正在观看世界杯，”她说。

contentParagraphs.en: Window closing to find survivors
contentParagraphs.zh: 关闭窗口寻找幸存者

contentParagraphs.en: These are the kinds of scenes playing out across La Guaira state, with people milling around in the streets after being left homeless, uncertain of what to do next.
contentParagraphs.zh: 拉瓜伊拉州正在上演这样的场景，人们无家可归后在街上闲逛，不确定下一步该做什么。

contentParagraphs.en: Nearly every building here shows damage. In some places there are deep cracks running through structures. In others, buildings lean like sinking ships.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这里几乎所有建筑物都受到损坏。在某些地方，建筑物上有很深的裂缝。在另一些地方，建筑物像正在下沉的船只一样倾斜。

contentParagraphs.en: High-rises remain standing but have lost entire facades, exposing interiors where life appears frozen in place—childhood bedrooms with Mickey Mouse sheets still on the bed, teddy bears still in place.
contentParagraphs.zh: 高楼依然矗立，但失去了整个外墙，暴露了内部，生活似乎被冻结在原地——童年的卧室，床上还放着米老鼠床单，泰迪熊仍然在原处。

contentParagraphs.en: The first 72 hours are crucial, when the chances of finding survivors are highest. That window is now closing.
contentParagraphs.zh: 前 72 小时至关重要，此时找到幸存者的机会最高。这个窗口现在正在关闭。

contentParagraphs.en: Residents transport a man rescued from the rubble three days after earthquakes struck, in La Guaira, Venezuela, Saturday, June 27, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 6 月 27 日星期六，委内瑞拉拉瓜伊拉，地震发生三天后，居民正在运送一名从废墟中获救的男子。

contentParagraphs.en: One of the immediate concerns is sanitation. The weather is humid and in some areas, there is already a strong smell of decay, raising fears of a growing public health crisis. Areas are being cordoned off to control access, while shelters are being opened for those who have lost their homes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 最紧迫的问题之一是卫生问题。天气潮湿，一些地区已经弥漫着强烈的腐烂气味，引发了人们对日益严重的公共卫生危机的担忧。一些地区被封锁以控制出入，同时为那些失去家园的人开放避难所。

contentParagraphs.en: Venezuela has been in crisis for more than a decade. The economy has collapsed, institutions and the health care system have weakened, and millions have left the country in the past decade.
contentParagraphs.zh: 委内瑞拉十多年来一直处于危机之中。过去十年，经济崩溃，机构和医疗保健系统削弱，数百万人离开该国。

contentParagraphs.en: Now this disaster adds another layer to that strain, raising questions about whether the state has the capacity to respond to a catastrophe of this scale.
contentParagraphs.zh: 现在，这场灾难又增加了压力，引发人们对国家是否有能力应对如此规模的灾难的疑问。
